name: actionlib
description:
The actionlib stack provides a standardized interface for
    interfacing with preemptable tasks. Examples of this include moving
    the base to a target location, performing a laser scan and returning
    the resulting point cloud, detecting the handle of a door, etc.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Vijay Pradeep, Mikael Arguedas

name: angles
description:
This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

name: bfl
description:
This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.
readme: BFL: BAYESIAN FILTERING LIBRARY Copyright Klaas Gadeyne dot gmail dot library free software; redistribute and/or modify terms GNU General Public License published Free Software Foundation; version License, option) version. program distributed hope WARRANTY; implied warranty MERCHANTABILITY FITNESS PURPOSE. GNU General Public License details. received copy GNU General Public License program; write Free Software Foundation, Temple Place Suite Boston, USA. library encoporates ideas software libraries: Scene (Andrew Davison). <http://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~ajd/Scene/> Bayes++ ACFR). <http://www.acfr.usyd.edu.au/technology/bayesianfilter/Bayes++.htm> CES programming library (Sebastian Thrun). <http://www-2.cs.cmu.edu/afs/cs.cmu.edu/user/thrun/public_html/papers/thrun.ces-tr.html> research Bayesian methods compliant motion problems <http://www.mech.kuleuven.be/pma/research/manip/default_en.phtml> important features Released GNU LGPL licence Wrapper matrix RNG libraries, favourite matrix library. wrappers exist matrix/RNG wrapper library LTIlib <http://ltilib.sourceforge.net/doc/homepage/index.shtml>: library algorithms data structures frequently image processing computer vision. NEWMAT <http://www.robertnz.net/nm_intro.htm> Matrix Library boost <http://www.boost.org/> RNG "Bayesian unifying Design". incorporate Bayesian filtering algorithm! filter schemes implemented. Standard EKF, IEKF Non-minimal State <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~tlefebvr/publicaties/BayesStat.ps.gz> Standard Particle filter (arbitrary proposal), BootstrapFilter (Proposal System Model PDF), Auxiliary Particle filter, Extended Kalman Particle Filter. details design ideas, poster library presented Valencia conference Bayesian Statistics, <http://people.mech.kuleuven.ac.be/~kgadeyne/doctoraat.html> filtering libraries home page <http://www.orocos.org/bfl> Tinne Laet Contributed tutorial website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/getting_started_guide/getting_started_guide.html> discusses construct filter bfl. Wim Meeussen Tinne Laet contributed installation guide website. <http://people.mech.kuleuven.be/~tdelaet/bfl_doc/installation_guide/installation_guide.html>

name: bond
description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

name: bond_core
description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing. The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

name: bondcpp
description:
C++ implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

name: bondpy
description:
Python implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

name: smclib
description:
The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.

name: test_bond
description: Contains tests for [[bond]], including tests for [[bondpy]] and [[bondcpp]].

name: calibration
description:
Provides a toolchain running through the robot calibration process. This
     involves capturing calibration data, estimating parameters, and
     then updating the URDF.

name: calibration_estimation
description:
Runs an optimization to estimate the a robot's kinematic parameters. This package is a
    generic rewrite of pr2_calibration_estimation.

name: calibration_launch
description:
This package contains a collection of launch files that can be helpful in configuring
    the calibration stack to run on your robot.

name: calibration_msgs
description:
This package defines messages for storing calibration samples
     to be used in full robot calibration procedures. This package
     is still unstable. Expect the messages to change.

description:
This package contains a script to generate calibration launch and configurationfiles for your robot.
    which is based on Michael Ferguson's calibration code

    Expect its APIs to change.

name: interval_intersection

Tools for calculating the intersection of interval messages coming
    in on several topics. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

name: joint_states_settler
description:
Provides a node that reports how long a subset of joints has been
     settled. That is, it calculates how long a set of joints has remained
     within a specified threshold. This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

name: laser_cb_detector
description:
Extracts checkerboard corners from a dense laser snapshot.
     This package is experimental and unstable. Expect its APIs to change.

name: monocam_settler
description:
Listens on a ImageFeatures topic, and waits for the data to settle.
     This package is experimental and unstable.
     Expect its APIs to change.

name: settlerlib
description:
Defines helper functions and routines that greatly help when trying to create a settler
    for a specific sensor channel. This package is experimental and unstable.
    Expect its APIs to change.

name: class_loader
description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

name: collada_parser
description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Collada robot
    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.
authors: Rosen Diankov, Kei Okada, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

authors: Tim Field, Rosen Diankov, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

    (retries, error handling, etc.) and interface

    timestamp_tools: Classes to help timestamp hardware events

name: timestamp_tools
description:
This package is currently for internal use only. Its API may change
    without warning in the future.  This package is deprecated.

name: dynamic_reconfigure
description:
This unary stack contains the dynamic_reconfigure package which provides a means to change
    node parameters at any time without having to restart the node.

name: ecl_command_line
description:
Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

name: ecl_concepts
description:
Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

name: ecl_containers
description:
The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed.

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

name: ecl_converters
description:
Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).

     They will come as the need arises.

name: ecl_core
description:
A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

name: ecl_core_apps
description:
This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.

name: ecl_devices
description: Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.

name: ecl_eigen
description: This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

name: ecl_exceptions
description:
Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.

name: ecl_filesystem
description: Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

name: ecl_formatters
description:
The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

name: ecl_geometry
description:
Any tools relating to mathematical geometry.
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

name: ecl_ipc
description:
Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it
  to be done consistently across platforms.

name: ecl_linear_algebra
description: Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

name: ecl_manipulators
description:
Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

name: ecl_math
description:
This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

name: ecl_mobile_robot
description:
Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

name: ecl_mpl
description:
Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

name: ecl_sigslots
description:
Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots,
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier
     and are multithread-safe.

name: ecl_statistics
description: Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

name: ecl_streams
description:
These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

name: ecl_threads
description:
This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded
     programming tools. These are usually different on different
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

name: ecl_time
description:
Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support

	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.

name: ecl_type_traits
description: Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.

name: ecl_utilities
description: Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

name: ecl_config
description:
These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types
     and functions.

name: ecl_console
description: Color codes for ansii consoles.

name: ecl_converters_lite
description:
These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new,
     templates or exceptions.

name: ecl_errors
description:
This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms.
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms,
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

name: ecl_io
description:
Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented
     with a cross platform set of functions.

name: ecl_lite
description: Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

name: ecl_sigslots_lite
description:
This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

name: ecl_time_lite
description:
Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

name: ecl
description: Metapackage bringing all of ecl together.

name: ecl_manipulation
description:
Includes basic manipulation related structures as well as a few 
    algorithms currently used at Yujin Robot.

name: ecl_manipulators
description:
Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just 
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

name: ecl_mobile_robot
description:
Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

name: ecl_navigation
description:
This stack aims to bring the common tools and algorithms needed to
    develop navigation algorithms, in particular slam. It does not focus on
    the end-point solution, rather the tools needed to create a variety of
    end-point solutions.

name: ecl_build
description:
Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

name: ecl_license
description:
Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.

name: ecl_tools
description: Tools and utilities for ecl development.

name: eigen_stl_containers
description:
This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

name: executive_smach
description:
This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

name: smach
description:
SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

name: smach_msgs
description:
this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

name: smach_ros
description:
The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with

name: executive_smach_visualization
description: This metapackage depends on the SMACH visualization tools.
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

name: smach_viewer
description:
The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the
maintainers: Jonathan Bohren, ROS Orphaned Package Maintainers

description: ROS-Industrial support for Fanuc manipulators (metapackage).

name: fcl
description: FCL is a collision checking library
readme: FCL Flexible Collision Library Linux Windows Coverage FCL library performing types proximity queries pair geometric models composed triangles. Collision detection: detecting models overlap, optionally, triangles overlap. Distance computation: computing minimum distance pair models, distance closest pair points. Tolerance verification: determining models closer tolerance distance. Continuous collision detection: detecting moving models overlap movement, optionally, time contact. Contact information: collision detection continuous collision detection, contact information (including contact normals contact points) returned optionally. FCL features interface Compilable linux win32 makefiles Microsoft Visual projects generated cmake) special topological constraints adjacency information required input models list model’s triangles Supported object shapes: box sphere ellipsoid capsule cone cylinder convex half-space plane mesh octree (optional, octrees represented octomap library http://octomap.github.com) Installation compiling FCL, Eigen libccd collision checking convex objects https://github.com/danfis/libccd) installed. libccd, compile github version zip file webpage, bug fixing included zipped version. optional libraries installed optional capability FCL. octree collision, install octomap library https://octomap.github.io/. CMakeLists.txt generate makefiles Linux Visual studio projects windows. command line, run mkdir build build cmake linux, compile code. windows, generate visual studio project compile code. Interfaces starting proximity computation, set geometry transform objects involving computation. geometry object represented mesh soup, set set mesh triangles vertice indices std::vector<Vector3f> vertices; std::vector<Triangle> triangles; code set vertices triangles BVHModel template class mesh geometry, default OBBRSS template typedef BVHModel<OBBRSSf> Model; std::shared_ptr<Model> geom std::make_shared<Model>(); add mesh data BVHModel structure geom->beginModel(); geom->addSubModel(vertices, triangles); geom->endModel(); transform object includes rotation translation: rotation matrix translation vector Matrix3f Vector3f code setting transform configured Transform3f pose Transform3f::Identity(); pose.linear() pose.translation() geometry transform, combine obtain collision object instance //geom geometry transform object std::shared_ptr<BVHModel<OBBRSSf>> geom Transform3f //Combine CollisionObjectf* obj CollisionObjectf(geom, objects set, perform proximity computation proximity queries FCL follow common pipeline: set query request data structure run query function request input. result returned query result data structure. collision checking, set CollisionRequest data structure, run collision function: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set collision request structure, default setting CollisionRequest request; result returned collision result structure CollisionResult result; perform collision test collide(o1, request, result); setting collision request, user easily choose return contact information slower) return binary collision results faster). distance computation, pipeline objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set distance request structure, default setting DistanceRequest request; result returned collision result structure DistanceResult result; perform distance test distance(o1, request, result); continuous collision, FCL requires goal transform initial transform included collision object data structure). pipeline distance/collision: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* goal transforms Transform3f tf_goal_o1 Transform3f tf_goal_o2 set continuous collision request structure, default setting ContinuousCollisionRequest request; result returned continuous collision result structure ContinuousCollisionResult result; perform continuous collision test continuousCollide(o1, tf_goal_o1, tf_goal_o2, request, result); FCL supports broadphase collision/distance groups objects avoid square complexity. collision, broadphase algorithm return collision pairs. distance, return pair minimum distance. FCL CollisionManager structure manage objects involving collision distance operations. Initialize collision manager group objects. FCL implementations CollisionManager. Generally, DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManager provide performance. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager1 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); Initialize collision manager group objects. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager2 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); add objects collision manager, BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObject() function add object std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects1 for(std::size_t objects1.size(); manager1->registerObject(objects1[i]); choose BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObjects() function add set objects std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects2 manager2->registerObjects(objects2); order collect information broadphase, CollisionManager requires settings: callback collision distance; intermediate data store information generated broadphase computation. convenience, FCL default callbacks satisfy call data satisfy collision distance queries. collision DefaultCollisionCallback DefaultCollisionData distance DefaultDistanceCallback DefaultDistanceData. default collision/distance data structs simply containers include request distance structures query type mentioned DefaultCollisionData collision_data; DefaultDistanceData distance_data; Setup managers, related initializing broadphase acceleration structure objects input manager1->setup(); manager2->setup(); Examples queries Collision query object groups collision numbers manager2->collide(manager1, &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); int n_contact_num collision_data.result.numContacts(); Distance query object groups minimum distance manager2->distance(manager1, &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); double min_distance distance_data.result.min_distance; collision query group manager1->collide(&collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); distance query group manager1->distance(&distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); Collision query object group entire group manager2->collide(objects1[0], &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); Distance query object group entire group manager2->distance(objects1[0], &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); examples, refer test folder: test_fcl_collision.cpp: provide examples collision test test_fcl_distance.cpp: provide examples distance test test_fcl_broadphase.cpp: provide examples broadphase collision/distance test test_fcl_frontlist.cpp: provide examples frontlist collision acceleration test_fcl_octomap.cpp: provide examples collision/distance computation octomap data data types.

name: tf
description:
tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

name: tf_conversions
description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

SVM existing model file, predicted center square good grasping point. good grasping points coordinates direction approach vectors published. DOWNLOAD CODE git HAF_GRASPING STARTED Start calculation server work), haf_client (small programming incl. class shows haf_grasping) visualization rviz: roslaunch haf_grasping haf_grasping_all.launch Publish path point cloud calculate grasp points object gripper approaching direction z-axis: rostopic pub /haf_grasping/input_pcd_rcs_path std_msgs/String "$(rospack find haf_grasping)/data/pcd2.pcd" (Alternatively, publish point cloud ros topic: /haf_grasping/depth_registered/single_cloud/points_in_lcs) EXPLANATION RVIZ VISUALIZATION RVIZ visualize point cloud frame (blue z-axis). Bigger rectangle: area heights grasp calculation rectangle: defines area grasps (grasp centers) searched. Long red line: closing direction finger gripper) Red/green spots: positions grasps tested current gripper roll (ignoring points calculation needed, data Green bars: grasps height bars grasp evaluation score higher Black arrow: grasp position approching direction parallel finger gripper) HAF-GRASPING CLIENT CODE EXPLAINDED calc_grasppoints_action_client.cpp subscribe point_cloud topic start callback point cloud code start goal (input point cloud) grasp calculation, send grasp action server receive result void CCalcGrasppointsClient::get_grasp_cb(const sensor_msgs::PointCloud2ConstPtr& pc_in) ROS_INFO("\nFrom calc_grasppoints_action_client: point cloud received"); create action client true client spin thread actionlib::SimpleActionClient ac("calc_grasppoints_svm_action_server", true); ROS_INFO("Waiting action server start."); wait action server start ac.waitForServer(); wait infinite time ROS_INFO("Action server started, sending goal."); send goal action haf_grasping::CalcGraspPointsServerGoal goal; goal.graspinput.input_pc *pc_in; goal.graspinput.grasp_area_center this->graspsearchcenter; set size grasp search area goal.graspinput.grasp_area_length_x this->grasp_search_size_x+14; goal.graspinput.grasp_area_length_y this->grasp_search_size_y+14; set max grasp calculation time goal.graspinput.max_calculation_time this->grasp_calculation_time_max; //send goal ac.sendGoal(goal); //wait action return bool finished_before_timeout ac.waitForResult(ros::Duration(50.0)); (finished_before_timeout) actionlib::SimpleClientGoalState state ac.getState(); boost::shared_ptr result ac.getResult(); ROS_INFO("Result: (*(result)).result.data.c_str()); ROS_INFO("Action finished: %s",state.toString().c_str()); ROS_INFO("Action finish time code PARAMETER SETTING number parameters set service call). name: hector_gazebo
description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

name: hector_gazebo_plugins
description:
hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.

name: hector_gazebo_thermal_camera
description: hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.

name: hector_gazebo_worlds
description: hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt

name: hector_sensors_gazebo
description: hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

name: hector_localization
description:
The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.

name: hector_pose_estimation
description: hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

name: hector_pose_estimation_core
description:
hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

name: message_to_tf
description:
message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).

name: hector_components_description
description: hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.

name: hector_models
description: hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.

name: hector_sensors_description
description: hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.

name: hector_xacro_tools
description: hector_xacro_tools

name: hector_compressed_map_transport
description: hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.

name: hector_geotiff
description: hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.

name: hector_geotiff_plugins
description: hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.

name: hector_imu_attitude_to_tf
description: hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.

name: hector_imu_tools
description: hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

name: hector_map_server
description:
hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

name: hector_map_tools
description:
hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

name: hector_mapping
description:
hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.

name: hector_marker_drawing
description: hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.

name: hector_nav_msgs
description: hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.

name: hector_slam
description: The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.

name: hector_slam_launch
description: hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.

name: hector_trajectory_server
description: hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.

name: hokuyo3d
description: ROS driver node for HOKUYO 3D LIDARs
readme: ROS driver node HOKUYO LIDARs package ROS driver node LIDARs VSSP protocol. VSSP protocol HOKUYO YVT-X002) HOKUYO YVT-35LX) supported.

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.

name: image_common
description: Common code for working with images in ROS.

name: image_transport
description:
image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

name: polled_camera
description:
polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

name: camera_calibration
description:
camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

name: depth_image_proc
description:
Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.

name: image_pipeline
description: image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.

name: image_proc
description: Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

name: image_view
description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.

name: stereo_image_proc
description: Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

name: compressed_depth_image_transport
description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

name: compressed_image_transport
description:
Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

name: image_transport_plugins
description:
A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

name: theora_image_transport
description:
Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

name: imagezero
description: ImageZero is a fast lossless image compression algorithm for RGB color photos.

Compared PNG, compresses bit PPM files times faster, decompresses PPM times faster, compression rate good PNG natural photos. ImageZero suited “flat” (computer generated) graphics grayscale images. code “early work progress”, image format, library API fully outlined contact author, AUTHORS file. feedback appreciated. code originally https://github.com/cfeck/imagezero modified build shared library part catkin package.

name: imagezero_image_transport
description: A plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images encoded with ImageZero.

name: imagezero_ros
description: A library that provides convenient methods for manipulating ROS images with ImageZero

description: ROS-Industrial core stack contains packages and libraries for supporing industrial systems

name: industrial_deprecated
description:
The Industrial deprecated package contains nodes, launch files, etc... that are slated for 
  deprecation.  This package is the last place something will end up before being deleted.  
  If you are missing a package/node and find it's contents here, then you should consider 
  a replacement.

name: industrial_msgs
description:
The industrial message package containes industrial specific messages 
	definitions. This package is part of the ROS-Industrial program.

name: industrial_robot_client
description:
industrial robot client contains generic clients for connecting 
     to industrial robot controllers with servers that adhere to the
     simple message protocol.

name: industrial_robot_simulator
description: The industrial robot simulator is a stand in for industrial robot driver node(s).  It adheres to the driver specification for industrial robot controllers.

name: industrial_utils
description: Industrial utils is a library package that captures common funcitonality for the ROS-Industrial distribution.

name: simple_message
description:
simple_message defines a simple messaging connection and protocol for communicating 
	with an industrial robot controller.  Additional handler and manager classes are 
	included for handling connection limited systems.  This package is part of the ROS-Industrial 
	program.

name: ivcon
description:
Mesh Conversion Utility

Used to generate '.iv' files from '.stl' files.  This package has not
been changed since 2001 and appears to be very stable.  We plan on
keeping this package in this revision for mesh conversions.  This
package is only available as a single source file for download.  There
are no local modifications to this package.

name: joy
description:
ROS driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend
readme: ROS Driver Generic Linux Joysticks joy package joy_node, node interfaces generic Linux joystick ROS. node publishes “Joy” message, current state joystick’s buttons axes. Supported Hardware node work joystick supported Linux. Published Topics joy (sensor_msgs/Joy): outputs joystick state. Device Selection parameters controlling device ~dev (string, default: “/dev/input/js0”) ~dev_name (string, default: (empty disabled)) ~dev_name empty, ~dev defines Linux joystick device read joystick events. ~dev_name defined, node enumerates joysticks, /dev/input/js*. joystick matching ~dev_name opened. joystick matches desired device device ~dev fallback. list names connected joysticks, invalid ~dev_name rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="*" output INFO]: joystick: LIS3LV02DL Accelerometer (/dev/input/js1). INFO]: joystick: Microsoft X-Box pad (/dev/input/js0). [ERROR]: find joystick Falling default device. node started device list. rosrun joy joy_node _dev_name:="Microsoft X-Box pad" Advanced Parameters ~deadzone (double, default: Amount joystick move considered off-center. parameter relative axis normalized means joystick move edge axis’s range axis output non-zero Linux deadzone processing, cases set ~autorepeat_rate (double, default: (disabled)) Rate joystick non-changing state resend previously message. ~coalesce_interval (double, default: Axis events received coalesce_interval (seconds) single ROS message. kernel sends axis motion separate event, coalescing greatly reduces rate messages option limit rate outgoing messages. Button events immediately avoid missing button presses. Information information Wiki page.

name: ps3joy
description:
Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.
    Driver for the Sony PlayStation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3
    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens
    for a connection on the HID ports, starts the joystick
    streaming data, and passes the data to the Linux uinput device
    so that it shows up as a normal joystick.
authors: Blaise Gassend, pascal <AT> pabr.org, Melonee Wise
readme: PlayStation Joystick Driver ROS package driver (SIXAXIS DUALSHOCK3) bluetooth joystick. driver reliable connection, access joystick’s accelerometers gyroscope. Linux’s native support joystick lacks functionality. Additional documentation: Troubleshooting Testing Instructions Bluetooth Device Compatibility Dependencies joystick libusb-dev bluez-5.37 Pairing instructions connect joystick bluetooth dongle computer connect joystick computer USB cable. Replace joystick’s mac address command: sudo rosrun ps3joy sixpair Starting joystick Run command rosrun ps3joy ps3joy.py Open terminal reboot bluez run joy sudo systemctl restart bluetooth rosrun joy joy_node Open terminal echo joy topic rostopic echo joy joystick /dev/input/js? check working jstest /dev/input/js? name: spacenav_node
description: ROS interface to the 3Dconnexion SpaceNavigator 6DOF joystick.
readme: Spacenav Nodes spacenav_node Published topics spacenav/offset (geometry_msgs/Vector3) Publishes linear component joystick’s position. Approximately normalized range spacenav/rot_offset(geometry_msgs/Vector3) Publishes angular component joystick’s position. Approximately normalized range spacenav/twist (geometry_msgs/Twist) Combines offset rot_offset single message. spacenav/joy (sensor_msgs/Joy) Outputs spacenav’s degrees freedom buttons joystick message. Parameters zero_when_static sets values spacenav moving static_count_threshold number polls needed device considered “static” static_trans_deadband sets translational deadband static_rot_deadband sets rotational deadband linear_scale sets scale linear output takes vector angular_scale sets scale angular output takes vector linear_scale angular_scale parameters vector arguements. easiest set parameters launch file <launch> <node pkg="spacenav_node" type="spacenav_node" name="$(anon spacenav_node)" output="screen"> <rosparam param="linear_scale">[.25, .25]</rosparam> <rosparam param="angular_scale">[.5, .5]</rosparam> </node> </launch> Running spacenav_node Running node straightforward $rosrun spacenav_node spacenav_node node publishing topics listed Examples Viewing output spacenav rostopic echo /spacenav/joy

name: wiimote
description:
The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.
authors: Andreas Paepcke, Melonee Wise, Mark Horn
readme: Wiimote Nodes Additional Documentation Testing procedure ROS Wiki Tutorials Teleop Wiimote wiimote_node.py Original Python version wiimote node. wiimote_node implementation designed focus reduced resource consumption. Parameters bluetooth_addr [str] Bluetooth address pairing. Default: device) pair_timeout [int] Pair timeout seconds. means timeout. Default: Differences Python Implementation “/wiimote/nunchuk” “/wiimote/classic” topics published Nunchuk Classic Controller connected wiimote Wiimote data polled controller data required publish topic subscriber. Calibration joysticks (Nunchuk Classic Controller) polled find current position assumed center. joysticks rest theoretical center position. Joysticks (Nunchuk Classic Controller) minimum maximum values start theoretical minimum/maximum values dynamically updated joystick full range values reached joysticks due physical limitations. adjust, full range reached joysticks. Joysticks (Nunchuk Classic Controller) center deadzone scaled based granularity joystick; python code assumed Connecting accessory (Nunchuk, Classic Controller, MotionPlus) automatically start calibration sequence.

default: maximum number seconds phrase continue stopping returning part phrase processed time limit reached ~phrase_threshold (Double, default: Minimum seconds speaking audio speaking audio phrase ~non_speaking_duration (Double, default: Seconds non-speaking audio sides recording ~duration (Double, default: Seconds waiting speech ~depth (Int, default: Depth audio signal ~n_channel (Int, default: Total number channels audio data mono, stereo) ~sample_rate (Int, default: Sample rate audio signal ~buffer_size (Int, default: Maximum buffer size store audio data speech recognition ~start_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/bell.ogg) Path sound file bell start audio ~recognized_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/message.ogg) Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. Usage usage: compress_imgs.py [--compressed-topics [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [--replace] [--no-progress-bar] input_bagfile Convert Image messages CompressedImage CompressedDepthImage positional arguments: input_bagfile input bagfile path optional arguments: show message exit output bagfile path --compressed-topics [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [COMPRESSED_TOPICS image topics compressed --replace --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools compress_imgs.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag-compressed.bag tf_static_to_tf.py Convert tf_static save rosbag. usage: tf_static_to_tf.py [--no-progress-bar] input_bagfile Convert tf_static save rosbag positional arguments: input_bagfile input bagfile path optional arguments: show message exit output bagfile path --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools tf_static_to_tf.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag Note jsk_topic_tools static_tf_republisher.py republish /tf_static rosbag file. tf_static_to_tf.py approach rewrite rosbag file. merge.py Merges bagfiles. name: cmd_vel_smoother
description: The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

name: jsk_teleop_joy
description: jsk_teleop_joy
readme: jsk_teleop_joy package Introduction package physical teleoperatoin joy sticks, game controllers MIDI instruments. Supported game controllers package supports game controllers: XBox360 controller Controller (Bluetooth) Controller (USB) order controller Bluetooth, instruction. Architecture jsk_teleop_joy jsk_teleop_joy main script joy_main.py) based plugin architecture. ROS packages plugins jsk_teleop_joy plugin represents function pose effector”. enable plugins choose plugin “Pushing Select Button”. jsk_teleop_joy plugins box VerboseStatus JoyPose6D JoyFootstep JoyFootstepPlanner JoyFootstepPlannerDemo JoyMoveIt JoyGoPos implement plugin write xml file define plugins. Export xml file ROS package manifest.xml package.xml. implement plugin Write xml file define plugins jsk_teleop_joy reads plugin definition xml file plugin.xml. xml <library> <class name="Foo" type="your_package.foo"> </class> </library> xml file <library> tag top level. define plugins <class> tag. attribute means plugin. choose plugins launch files type attribute means python class plugin. jsk_teleop_joy instantiate plugin class thie type Export xml file ROS package export xml file <export> tag <jsk_teleop_joy> tag manifest.xml package.xml. files, manifest.xml, package.xml, good examples. Implement plugin Finally, implement jsk_teleop_joy plugin. VerboseStatus Plugin good implement plugin. __init__ method plugins required inherits jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin call JSKJoyPlugin.__init__ __init__ constructor. class VerboseStatus(jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin): def __init__(self): jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin.__init__(self, 'VerbosePlugin') joyCB method time jsk_teleop_joy receives /joy message, calls joyCB method active plugin. def joyCB(self, status, history): rospy.loginfo('analog left (status.left_analog_x, status.left_analog_y)) argument joyCB instance JoyStatus. JoyStatus XboxStatus, PS3Status PS3WiredStatus. means latest message /joy. classes provide interface don’t care controller user hand, argument joyCB (history), sequence JoyStatus. instance StatusHistory. means hisotry JoyStatus. SELECT button jsk_teleop_joy system occupies SELECT button SELECT button plugin. SELECT button switching plugins. MIDI controllers interactive_midi_config.py configure MIDI devices script interactively. movie Connect MIDI device. script asks device, choose device push buttons/slide faders order sensor_msgs/Joy. script maps buttons sensor_msgs/Joy/axes sensor_msgs/Joy/buttons type quit. configure saved /tmp/midi.yaml. Troubleshooting: Ubuntu18.04 pygame2.0.1 installed pip, python "import pygame.midi; pygame.midi.init()" returns error ALSA lib conf.c:3558:(snd_config_hooks_call) open shared library libasound_module_conf_pulse.so (/usr/lib/alsa-lib/libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: open shared object file: file directory) ALSA lib seq.c:935:(snd_seq_open_noupdate) Unknown SEQ default case, pygame apt pip uninstall pygame apt-get install python-pygame RECOMMENDED: pip installed pygame, symbolic link /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/alsa-lib /usr/lib/alsa-lib: RECOMMED midi_write.py order control LEDs active faders, output MIDI commands computer. script midi_write.py helps dig command save configuration yaml file option. midi_config_player.py script publishes sensor_msgs/Joy /joy based yaml file configured interactive_midi_config.py midi_write.py. configs directory includes yaml files MIDI devices.

description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. script convert multiple message types spectrum. Publishing topics audio_to_spectrum.py. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data convert spectrum published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample input data FFT. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. spectrum_filter.py script filter spectrum. Publishing topics ~output (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Filtered spectrum data (low_cut_freq-high_cut_freq). Subscribing topics ~input (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Original spectrum data. Parameters ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate data generation original spectrum data. ~high_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit maximum frequency output spectrum. ~low_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit minimum frequency output spectrum. spectrum_to_spectrogram.py script convert spectrum spectrogram. Publishing topics ~spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image) Spectrogram data, concatenation spectrum time series. Image format Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~image_height (Int, default: Number vertical (frequency axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~image_width (Int, default: Number horizontal (time axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~spectrogram_period (Double, default: Period store spectrum data create spectrogram topic. ~publish_rate (Double, default: image_width spectrogram_period) Publish rate spectrogram topic. audio_amplitude_plot.py script publish audio amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Audio amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size sound input plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate audio amplitude image topic. data_amplitude_plot.py Generalized version audio_amplitude_plot.py. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Data amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data amplitude plot published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size input data plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate data amplitude image topic. spectrum_plot.py script publish frequency amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Frequency amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~min_amp (Double, default: Minimum amplitude plot. ~max_amp (Double, default: Maximum amplitude plot. ~queue_size (Int, default: Queue size spectrum subscriber. ~max_rate (Double, default: Maximum publish rate frequency amplitude plot image. Setting reduces CPU load. means maximum limit.

name: sound_classification
description: The sound_classification package
readme: Sound Classification ROS package classify sound stream. Contents Setup Usage Quick demo Setup Install ROS. Ubuntu Ubuntu Create workspace mkdir ~/sound_classification_ws/src ~/sound_classification_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition.git rosdep install --from-paths --ignore-src catkin build sound_classification source ~/sound_classification_ws/devel/setup.bash Install packages. cuda cupy needed chainer. installation guide JSK GPU highly recommended. Usage Check microphone parameters. device, n_channel, bitdepth mic_sampling_rate bash commands params device. card device device:="hw:0,0" arecord List CAPTURE Hardware Devices card PCH [HDA Intel PCH], device ALC293 Analog [ALC293 Analog] Subdevices: Subdevice subdevice n_channel, bitdepth sample_rate, Note sources means input microphone) sinks means output speaker) pactl list short sources alsa_input.pci-0000_00_1f.3.analog-stereo module-alsa-card.c s16le SUSPENDED Pass params launch file arguments launching `device:=hw:0,0 n_channel:=2 bitdepth:=16 mic_sampling_rate:=44100`). `/audio` topic computer publish `/audio`, set `use_microphone:=false` launch file launching. Save environmental noise train_data/noise.npy. subtracting noise, spectrograms clear. script, give sound sensor. update noise data everytime sound recognition, environmental sound differs everytime. noise samples roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Publish audio spectrum spectrogram topics. set max/min frequency included spectrum high_cut_freq/low_cut_freq args audio_to_spectrogram.launch. gui:=true, spectrum spectrogram visualized. roslaunch sound_classification audio_to_spectrogram.launch gui:=true spectrogram quiet environment. Horiozntal axis time Vertical axis frequency |Spectrogram noise subtraction|Spectrogram noise subtraction| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824253-d9e6df80-c0c8-11ea-8946-ca1367c1b1b0.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86824246-d81d1c00-c0c8-11ea-8c13-dc9660e89ea0.png)| Collect spectrogram classify. volume exceeds threshold, save spectrogram train_data/original_spectrogram/TARGET_CLASS. rosbag stream sound sources. Rosbag version (Recommended) recommend rosbag collect spectrograms. rosbag easy save_sound.launch parameters. target_class:=TARGET_CLASS, set class target sound. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG, save spectrograms rosbag. default, rosbag paused Press ‘Space’ key terminal start playing rosbag. rosbag ends, press ‘Ctrl-c’ terminate. newly saved spectrograms appended existing spectrograms. change threshold sound saving threshold:=xxx. smaller easily sound saved. Save audio rosbag roslaunch sound_classification record_audio_rosbag.launch filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG play rosbag collecting data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 setting `threshold:=0` `save_when_sound:=false`, collect spectrogram sound". play rosbag collecting no-sound data export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://localhost:11311 roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false Stream version Recommended) collect spectrogram audio topic stream. `use_rosbag:=true`. args rosbag version. roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch save_when_sound:=true target_class:=TARGET_CLASS threshold:=0.5 save_data_rate:=5 Create dateaset chainer saved spectrograms. data augmentation executed. --number means maximum images class dataset. rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Visualize dataset. train arg train dataset (augmented dataset), test arg test dataset. spectrograms dataset visualized random order. rosrun sound_classification visualize_dataset.py test train/test Train dataset. Default model NIN (Recommended). vgg16, pretrained weights VGG16 downloaded scripts/VGG_ILSVRC_16_layers.npz time run script. rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sounds. takes seconds neural network weights loaded. use_rosbag:=true filename:=PATH_TO_ROSBAG classify sound rosbag. roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch fix class names' color classification result image order class names <rosparam> target_names: chip_bag] </rosparam> classification result: |no_sound|applause|voice| |![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828259-ed487980-c0cd-11ea-9f51-7ccb0bc93321.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828249-ec174c80-c0cd-11ea-854c-da1b0fa08e33.png)|![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/19769486/86828260-ede11000-c0cd-11ea-9b4e-94ee7b1c1a5f.png)| Quick demo Sound classification demo laptop’s built-in microphone. create dataset rosbag files sample_rosbag/ directory. Classification gif Commands Setup environment write Microphone settings Usage section) Save environmental noise roslaunch sound_classification save_noise.launch Collect spectrograms sample rosbags. Press ‘Space’ start rosbag. no_sound class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/no_sound.bag target_class:=no_sound threshold:=0 save_when_sound:=false applause class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/applause.bag target_class:=applause threshold:=0.5 voice class roslaunch sound_classification save_sound.launch use_rosbag:=true filename:=$(rospack find sound_classification)/sample_rosbag/voice.bag target_class:=voice threshold:=0.5 Create dataset rosrun sound_classification create_dataset.py --number Train (takes minites) rosrun sound_classification train.py --epoch Classify sound roslaunch sound_classification classify_sound.launch

name: roseus_smach
description:
roseus_smach

     * Euslisp state machine class. it will be moved.
     * Message publisher for visualizing current state by smach_viewer.
     * Simple pickle dump script for debugging state machine.
     * Execute state machine as a action server.
readme: roseus_smach package includes euslisp implementation state machine smach. requirements roseus smach smach_viewer Optional visualization. install package manually: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-smach-viewer sample Sample codes sample directory. rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l simple state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-simple) nested state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-nested) state machine userdata rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-userdata) sample/parallel-state-machine-sample.l state machine parallel action execution rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach parallel-state-machine-sample.l (demo) Writing Simple Smach(state-machine) codes #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (setq count (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) (defun smach-simple (instance state-machine :init))) (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) (send :add-node (instance state :init :BAR 'func-bar)) goal-states generated method (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) select node start-node (send :start-state :FOO) nodes selected symbol (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) (send :add-transition :FOO :outcome4 :outcome2) (send :add-transition :BAR :FOO :outcome2) (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) Code Explained (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) lines define functions hooked state. Note functions called argument, alist declared user-data arguments, return equal transition action) state. (instance state-machine :init)) line creates state-machine instance binds (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) line creates state add node state machine. create state instance, state hook state function. lisp object state names. case, state :FOO hooked func-foo. (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) (send :start-state :FOO) lines define goal state(s) start state(s) state machine. create multiple start states list state, define multiple goal state. Goal state(s) returns Note state machine state. goal state considered outcome state machine. (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) line defines transition. arguments :add-transition method OUTCOME. line means “adding transition FOO BAR, FOO node returns outcome1”. (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) line sets execute state machine. order view check progress smach_viewer, function exec-state-machine Writing Nested Smach codes add child state-machine node state-machine. #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (defun smach-nested ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) state instance include state-machine function (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) node instance args :add-node, :start-state, :add-transition ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) sm-top (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Code Explained #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") previous line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) line creates state-machine instance. sm-sub state machine acting state node) sm-top. easy understand state machine function: state machine called, executed, state machine processing eventually returns goal state return function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) sm-sub instance hooked node sm-top line. line add state machine state function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) lines define node, goal-state, start-state, transition sm-top. (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) Remember goal state(s) state-machine returns outcome return sm-sub goal state(s). goal state sm-sub :outcome4, return sm-sub :outcome4. line adds transition :outcome5 node returns :outcome4. ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) lines define behavior sm-sub detail previous simple state machine Note (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) defines goal state, defines return state machine. (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Finally, sm-top executed Writing Simple Smach (make-state-machine) make-state-machine function easy-way define simple state machine. requires graph-list, func-map, initial-state, goal-states arguments. simple state machine written (defun smach-simple2 (setq (make-state-machine define graph, list (<from-node> <transition> <to-node>) <transition> corresponds node returns nil. '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) define function map '((:foo 'func-foo) foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2 (:bar 'func-bar)) bar returns :outcome2 initial state '(:foo) goal state '(:outcome4))))) node :foo :bar :outcome4 terminate node. node corresponds 'func-foo 'func-bar functions. function 'func-foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2. function 'func-bar returns :outcome2. (:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) means :foo returns :outcome2, transit :outcome4. (:foo :outcome1 :bar) means :foo returns :outcome1, transit :bar. (:bar :outcome2 :foo) means :bar returns :outcome2, transit :foo. simplify state machine definition, recommend users t/nil return node, users able (:foo :outcome4) graph definition. (defun smach-simple3 (setq (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome4) (:foo :bar) (:bar :foo)) '((:foo '(lambda (&rest args) (cond count (incf count) nil) foo returns nil times returns (:bar '(lambda (&rest args) bar returns '(:foo) '(:outcome4))))) tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple2)))" roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple3)))" check state machine behavior rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py` Writing Nested Smach (make-state-machine) write nested state machine make-state-machie function. add state-machine function maps, added node (defun smach-simple-nested (sm-top sm-sub) (setq sm-sub (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) transitions (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) '((:foo 'func-foo) function maps (:bar 'func-bar)) '(:foo) initial '(:outcome4) goal (setq sm-top (make-state-machine '((:bas :outcome3 transitions :outcome4 :outcome5)) `((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps ,sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" '(:bas) initial '(:outcome5) goal sm-top)) roseus write '((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" careful add machine. `(backquote) (comma). tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple-nested)))"

name: leap_motion
description: ROS driver for the Leap Motion gesture sensor
authors: Florian Lier, Mirza Shah, Isaac IY Saito
readme: ROS LEAP MOTION ROS driver Leap Motion Controller REQUIREMENTS ROS Kinetic newer version installed device Leap Motion SDK Linux. Overview interfaces MoveGroupInterface move arm move_group action. PlanningSceneInterface add/remove collision attached objects. set colors objects. PickPlaceInterface interface pick place actions. MoveGroupInterface MoveGroupInterface easy import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") MoveGroupInterface("planning_group_name", "base_link") values base_link, planning group called “planning_group_name”. PlanningSceneInterface PlanningSceneInterface easily insert remove objects MoveIt! planning scene. Starting version module newer service-based approach apply planning scene updates, robust publishing messages topics asynchronously. import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") create planning scene interface, provide root link PlanningSceneInterface("base_link") add cube size, base_link frame p.addCube("my_cube", remove cube p.removeCollisionObject("my_cube") reason prefer service, simply set “use_service” false add/remove calls. Calling waitForSync republish messages needed synchronize planning scene script MoveIt. p.addCube("my_cube", use_service=False) p.addCube("my_other_cube", use_service=False) p.waitForSync() PickPlaceInterface import rospy moveit_python import moveit_msgs.msg import Grasp, PlaceLocation rospy.init_node("moveit_py") provide arm group gripper group names takes parameter "plan_only" defaults False PickPlaceInterface("arm", "gripper") Grasp() fill setup object named object_name PlanningSceneInterface p.pickup("object_name", support_name "supporting_surface") PlaceLocation() fill p.place("object_name" goal_is_eef True, support_name "supporting_surface") Migration moveit_utils GraspingInterface renamed PickPlaceInterface pick/place functions return entire action result, moveit_utils returned error_code. access error code returned, result.error_code.val ObjectManager renamed PlanningSceneInterface remove function removeCollisionObject removeAttachedObject ArmInterface renamed MoveGroupInterface

name: default_cfg_fkie
description:
The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

name: master_discovery_fkie
description:
Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

name: master_sync_fkie
description:
Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

name: multimaster_fkie
description:
The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.

name: multimaster_msgs_fkie
description: The messages required by multimaster packages.

name: node_manager_fkie
description:
Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.
