name: base_local_planner
description: This package provides implementations of the Trajectory Rollout and Dynamic Window approaches to local robot navigation on a plane. Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Perko, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: carrot_planner
description: This planner attempts to find a legal place to put a carrot for the robot to follow. It does this by moving back along the vector between the robot and the goal point.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Sachin Chitta, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: clear_costmap_recovery
description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by reverting the costmaps used by the navigation stack to the static map outside of a given area.

name: costmap_2d
description:
This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: dwa_local_planner
description:
This package provides an implementation of the Dynamic Window Approach to
        local robot navigation on a plane. Given a global plan to follow and a
        costmap, the local planner produces velocity commands to send to a mobile
        base. This package supports any robot who's footprint can be represented as
        a convex polygon or cicrle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters
        that can be set in a launch file. The parameters for this planner are also
        dynamically reconfigurable. This package's ROS wrapper adheres to the
        BaseLocalPlanner interface specified in the

name: nav_core
description: This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.

name: navfn
description:
navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: navigation
description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor
        streams, and a goal pose and outputs safe velocity commands that are sent
        to a mobile base.
readme: ROS Navigation Stack navigation stack takes information odometry, sensor streams, goal pose outputs safe velocity commands mobile base. Related stacks: http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_tutorials http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_experimental

name: robot_pose_ekf
description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.

name: rotate_recovery
description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.

name: voxel_grid
description: voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

name: nav2d
description: Meta-Package containing modules for 2D-Navigation

This package holds a collection of plugins for the RobotNavigator, that provide
    different cooperative exploration strategies for a team of mobile robots.

name: nav2d_karto

Graph-based Simultaneous Localization and Mapping module.

name: nav2d_localizer

name: nav2d_msgs

name: nav2d_navigator
description:
This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

name: nav2d_operator
description:
The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

name: nav2d_remote
description:
This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.

name: realtime_tools
description:
Contains a set of tools that can be used from a hard
    realtime thread, without breaking the realtime behavior.  The
    tools currently only provides the realtime publisher, which makes
    it possible to publish messages to a ROS topic from a realtime
    thread. We plan to add a basic implementation of a realtime
    buffer, to make it possible to get data from a (non-realtime)
    topic callback into the realtime loop. Once the lockfree buffer is
    created, the realtime publisher will start using it, which will
    result in major API changes for the realtime publisher (removal of
    all lock methods).
readme: realtime_tools ros_control documentation ros.org Build Status

   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

name: teb_local_planner
description:
The teb_local_planner package implements a plugin
	to the base_local_planner of the 2D navigation stack.
	The underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes
	the robot's trajectory with respect to trajectory execution time,
	separation from obstacles and compliance with kinodynamic constraints at runtime.
readme: teb_local_planner ROS Package teb_local_planner package implements plugin base_local_planner navigation stack. underlying method called Timed Elastic Band locally optimizes robot’s trajectory respect trajectory execution time, separation obstacles compliance kinodynamic constraints runtime. Refer http://wiki.ros.org/teb_local_planner information tutorials. Build status jade-devel branch: ROS Buildfarm: Travis: Papers Describing Approach Rösmann Feiten Wösch Hoffmann Bertram. Trajectory modification dynamic constraints autonomous robots. Proc. German Conference Robotics, Germany, Munich, Rösmann Feiten Wösch Hoffmann Bertram. Efficient trajectory optimization sparse model. Proc. IEEE European Conference Mobile Robots, Spain, Barcelona, Sept. Rösmann Hoffmann Bertram. Planning Multiple Robot Trajectories Distinctive Topologies, Proc. IEEE European Conference Mobile Robots, Lincoln, Sept. Videos left videos presents features package shows examples simulation real robot situations. spoken explanations included audio track video. demonstrates features introduced version (supporting car-like robots costmap conversion). watch left License teb_local_planner package licensed BSD license. depends ROS packages, listed package.xml. BSD licensed. third-party dependencies included licensed terms: Eigen, MPL2 license, http://eigen.tuxfamily.org libg2o licensed BSD, enabled csparse_extension licensed LGPL3+, https://github.com/RainerKuemmerle/g2o. CSparse included part SuiteSparse collection, http://www.suitesparse.com. Boost, Boost Software License, http://www.boost.org packages included distributed hope WARRANTY; implied warranty MERCHANTABILITY FITNESS PURPOSE. licenses details. Requirements Install dependencies (listed package.xml CMakeLists.txt file) rosdep: rosdep install teb_local_planner

name: trac_ik
description:
The ROS packages in this repository were created to provide an improved
    alternative Inverse Kinematics solver to the popular inverse Jacobian
    methods in KDL.  TRAC-IK handles joint-limited chains better than KDL
    without increasing solve time.

name: trac_ik_lib
description:
TRAC-IK is a faster, significantly more reliable drop-in replacement for
    KDL's pseudoinverse Jacobian solver.

    The TRAC-IK library has a very similar API to KDL's IK solver calls,
    except that the user passes a maximum time instead of a maximum number of
    search iterations.  Additionally, TRAC-IK allows for error tolerances to
    be set independently for each Cartesian dimension (x,y,z,roll,pitch.yaw).
readme: TRAC-IK kinematics solver built trac_ik_lib library tested ROS Indigo Catkin). headers shared objects package linked user programs. package part ROS Noetic binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-noetic-trac-ik package part ROS Indigo/Jade binaries: sudo apt-get install ros-jade-trac-ik requires Ubuntu packages NLOpt Libraries installed ros-indigo-nlopt packages proper headers). running sudo apt-get install libnlopt-dev trusty standard Ubuntu distros. Alternatively, run ```rosdep update rosdep install trac_ik_lib KDL KDL::ChainFkSolverPos_recursive fk_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverVel_pinv vik_solver(chain); KDL::ChainJntToJacSolver jac_solver(chain); KDL::ChainIkSolverPos_NR_JL ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, fk_solver, vik_solver, int num_iterations, double error); int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: timeout seconds iterations set called loop timer. NOTE: error acceptable purposes TRAC-IK: #include <trac_ik/trac_ik.hpp> TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(KDL::Chain chain, KDL::JntArray lower_joint_limits, KDL::JntArray upper_joint_limits, double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); TRAC_IK::TRAC_IK ik_solver(string base_link, string tip_link, string URDF_param="/robot_description", double timeout_in_secs=0.005, double error=1e-5, TRAC_IK::SolveType type=TRAC_IK::Speed); NOTE: arguments constructors optional. type Speed: returns quickly solution Distance: runs full timeout_in_secs, returns solution minimizes SSE seed Manip1: runs full timeout, returns solution maximizes sqrt(det(J*J^T)) product singular values Jacobian) Manip2: runs full timeout, returns solution minimizes ratio min max singular values Jacobian. int ik_solver.CartToJnt(KDL::JntArray joint_seed, KDL::Frame desired_end_effector_pose, KDL::JntArray& return_joints, KDL::Twist tolerances); NOTE: CartToJnt succeeded NOTE: tolerances effector pose optional, default ABS() values set tolerances -tol..0..+tol Cartesian dimensions effector pose.

name: trac_ik_python
description:
The trac_ik_python package contains a python wrapper using SWIG
  for trac_ik_lib
readme: trac_ik_python Python wrapper TRAC library SWIG generate bindings. send questions Sam Pfeiffer usage Upload robot param server: roslaunch pr2_description upload_pr2.launch IK’s: #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") seed_state ik_solver.number_of_joints ik_solver.get_ik(seed_state, Returns: play bounds call: #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") seed_state ik_solver.number_of_joints ik_solver.get_ik(seed_state, bounds Rotation bounds Returns: coordinate frame poses base frame, check links/joints #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") ik_solver.base_link 'torso_lift_link' ik_solver.tip_link 'r_wrist_roll_link' ik_solver.joint_names ('r_shoulder_pan_joint', 'r_shoulder_lift_joint', 'r_upper_arm_roll_joint', 'r_elbow_flex_joint', 'r_forearm_roll_joint', 'r_wrist_flex_joint', 'r_wrist_roll_joint') ik_solver.link_names ('r_shoulder_pan_link', 'r_shoulder_lift_link', 'r_upper_arm_roll_link', 'r_upper_arm_link', 'r_elbow_flex_link', 'r_forearm_roll_link', 'r_forearm_link', 'r_wrist_flex_link', 'r_wrist_roll_link') initialize string URDF default takes /robot_description parameter param server): #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import URDF urdf_str rospy.get_param('/robot_description') ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link", urdf_string=urdf_str) check modify joint limits: #!/usr/bin/env python trac_ik_python.trac_ik import ik_solver IK("torso_lift_link", "r_wrist_roll_link") lower_bound, upper_bound ik_solver.get_joint_limits() lower_bound: upper_bound: ik_solver.set_joint_limits([0.0]* ik_solver.number_of_joints, upper_bound) Extra notes Python wrapper SWIG extended languages. You’ll extra output instantiating class WARN] root link base_footprint inertia URDF, KDL support root link inertia. workaround, add extra dummy link URDF. finishing program you’ll error apparently): terminate called throwing instance 'boost::exception_detail::clone_impl<boost::exception_detail::error_info_injector<boost::lock_error> boost: mutex lock failed pthread_mutex_lock: Invalid argument Aborted (core dumped) final note didn’t simplify letting Pose/PoseStamped messages input, transforming coordinate frames, adding kinematics, simple.

name: visp_auto_tracker
description:
Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode of Flash code pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow

name: hector_object_tracker
description:
hector_object_tracker is the core package of hector_worldmodel. It listens to percept message from
     detectors (e.g. heat signatures of persons or recognitions of other objects of interest in the
     scene) and fuses all information to a single worldmodel state. Objects will be tracked over time
     and their states can be influenced by a couple of services.

     The percept to object association problem is solved either automatically based on the Mahalanobis
     distance, or a unique object_id can be given in the percept message.

     If a hector_nav_msgs/GetDistanceToObstacle service is available, the object_tracker can optionally
     deduce the depth of objects in the scene by projection to the nearest obstacle (wall).

name: hector_worldmodel_msgs
description:
hector_worldmodel_msgs is a message package to comes with the hector_worldmodel stack.
     The messages can be used to send percepts from images (hector_worldmodel_msgs/ImagePercept) or other sources
     (hector_worldmodel_msgs/PosePercept) to the hector_object_tracker node. The tracker publishes model updates as
     hector_worldmodel_msgs/Object messages and latches the whole model state as a hector_worldmodel_msgs/ObjectModel message.

name: msp
description: Implementation of the MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP)
readme: MultiWii Cleanflight Baseflight Communication Library library implements MultiWii Serial Protocol (MSP) communicating MultiWii Cleanflight flight controller serial device. defines low-level API sending+encoding receiving+decoding MSP messages, high-level API subscriber pattern periodically request data call callback functions message received. communication tested MultiWii Arduino Nano achieve update rate approximately cycle time Betaflight Naze32 Rev6 update rates max. Installation Test Linux (Ubuntu Debian) install asio ninja: sudo apt install --no-install-recommends ninja-build libasio-dev check source code cmake compile: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release cmake --build build run program path serial device: ./msp_read_test /dev/ttyUSB0 Windows Requirements CMake asio: download asio-1.20.0.zip extract header files, C:\asio-1.20.0 Visual Build Tools: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/de/downloads/ Build Test open Developer Command Prompt Visual Studio change directory checked msp run: cmake build -GNinja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DASIO_ROOT=C:\asio-1.20.0 cmake --build build test serial device: msp_read_test.exe Hardware Setup connect Arduino Naze32 boards built-in USB-to-Serial connector direct serial connection (RX->TX, TX->RX, GND->GND, VCC->VCC). option preferable high transfer rates, direct connection exposed transmission errors. MultiWii MSP update rate determined variable LOOP_TIME config.h #define LOOP_TIME sets loop time update rate Arduino boards integrated USB-to-serial converter auto-reset serial connection established. send messages opening connection request send response. prevent delay opening connection sending messages, disabling Data Terminal Ready (DTR) line, stty /dev/ttyUSB0 -hupcl. Cleanflight Betaflight change update rate serial task range set serial_update_rate_hz=2000, save increase serial baudrate Ports configuration tab Sending Receiving commands activate feature RX_MSP GUI: Configuration Receiver Receiver Mode MSP input CLI: execute feature RX_MSP Beginning Cleanflight Betaflight MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets insufficient flash memory naze32). verify MSP_RC messages return empty list channels. activate MSP_SET_RAW_RC messages targets, add #define USE_RX_MSP target.h, src/main/target/NAZE/target.h. FlightContoller API FlightContoller API user send/receive messages flight controller. Messages queried periodically, demand. Instantiation Instantiate setup FlightController class: #include <FlightController.hpp> fcu::FlightController fcu; connection setup fcu.connect("/dev/ttyUSB0", Periodic request messages Messages queried periodically handled automatically subscription. class method stand-alone function. Class method pattern Define class holds callback functions process information received message. function signature void <callback_name>(const <child_of_msp::Message>& class App public: void onStatus(const msp::Status& status) std::cout<<status; void onImu(const msp::Imu& imu) std::cout<<imu; Instantiate class callbacks register FCU desired update rate seconds: App app; fcu.subscribe(&App::onStatus, &app, fcu.subscribe(&App::onImu, &app, Requests processed flight controller fast important note MultiWii FCU processed single message cycle. subscribed messages share effective bandwidth messages Lambda pattern FlightController::subscribe method restricted callbacks return void argument const msp::Message&. order call method doesn’t match signature additional information), wrap non-compliant method lambda matches expected signature. auto callback [](const msp::msg::RawImu& imu){ ImuSI subclass msp::Message, suitable callback argument std::cout msp::msg::ImuSI(imu, fcu.subscribe<msp::msg::RawImu>(callback, Manual sending messages Additional messages periodically dispatched method bool sendMessage(msp::Message &message, const double timeout instantiate instance object matching message send provide method optional timeout. timeout paramter method wait (possibly response received flight controller. strictly positive limit waiting interval. msp::Status status; (fcu.sendMessage(status) status values returned flight controller msp::SetCalibrationData calibration; calibration.acc_zero_x calibration.acc_zero_y calibration.acc_zero_x calibration.acc_gain_x calibration.acc_gain_y calibration.acc_gain_z (fcu.sendMessage(calibration) calibration data message return data, values unchanged message type data response flight controller, instance message sendMessage call values unpacked flight controller’s response.

License: 3-clause BSD License Copyright Jose Luis Blanco jlblanco@ual.es (University Almeria) collaborators refer MVSim SoftwareX paper ArXiV preprint) gentle introduction project architecture. cite MVSim work, @article{blanco2023mvsim, title {MultiVehicle Simulator (MVSim): Lightweight dynamics simulator multiagents mobile robotics research}, journal {SoftwareX}, volume pages year issn doi {https://doi.org/10.1016/j.softx.2023.101443}, url {https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2352711023001395}, author {José-Luis Blanco-Claraco Borys Tymchenko Francisco José Mañas-Alvarez Fernando Cañadas-Aránega Ángel López-Gázquez José Carlos Moreno} ROSCon talk Spanish talk English slides subtitles (slides Installation installation documentation details options. easiest install ROS ROS sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-mvsim jump steps launch demo worlds. Demo videos Build matrix status Distro Build dev Build releases Stable version ROS Noetic amd64 arm64 armhf ROS Humble amd64 arm64 ROS Jazzy amd64 arm64 ROS Rolling amd64 arm64 EOL distro Stable version ROS Melodic ROS Foxy ROS Iron Docs Main documentation site clone repository, remember checkout git submodules git clone https://github.com/MRPT/mvsim.git --recursive Launch demos steps Standalone: mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_warehouse.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/demo_2robots.world.xml mvsim launch mvsim_tutorial/test_mesh.world.xml ROS roslaunch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch ROS ros2 launch mvsim demo_warehouse.launch.py ros2 launch mvsim demo_depth_camera.launch.py Main features Lightweight memory, CPU library requirements. Fully configurable .xml “world” files. Headless mode, suitable dockerized environments. World maps: Occupancy gridmaps: input images MRPT binary maps icp-slam, rbpf-slam, Elevation meshes. Vehicle models: Differential driven wheel drive). Ackermann steering (kinematic dynamic steering, mechanical drive models). Ackermann steering mechanical differentials full grade. Sensors: Lidars: Robots bodies, RGB cameras Depth cameras Interface vehicles: Custom Python interface, ROS. Choose Raw access forces motor torques. Twist commands internal controllers).

readme: nao_moveit_config MoveIt! config package generated MoveIt! wizard. requires NAO model https://github.com/ros-naoqi/nao_robot/tree/master/nao_description binary package ros-indigo-nao-description moveit package run remote computer robot. URDF Textures moveit package based textured nao: https://github.com/ros-naoqi/nao_meshes urdf generation configuration files https://github.com/ros-naoqi/nao_robot Compile packages nao_moveit_config package compilation running rospack profile dcm gazebo packages compile nodes. order compile nao_dcm packages, set environment variable AL_DIR path naoqiSDK-c++ computer. run usual {.sourceCode .bash} catkin_make ready play nao Run MoveIt robot run moveit package unconnected robot attached robot (real simulated): standalone execution {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch nao_moveit_config demo.launch launching demo, robot MoveIt! GUI {width=”100.0%”} real robot launch real nao nao_dcm_bringup (https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/nao_dcm_robot) nao_control (https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/nao_virtual) set NAO_IP environment variable robot's address: {.sourceCode .bash} export NAO_IP= Launch DCM bringup: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch nao_dcm_bringup nao_dcm_H25_bringup_remote.launch Wait ready, run: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch nao_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch simulated robot Gazebo) MoveIt! Gazebo simulation, nao\_dcm\_bringup (<https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/nao_dcm_robot>) nao\_control (<https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/nao_virtual>) nao\_gazebo\_plugin (<https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/nao_virtual>) Launch Gazebo, {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch nao_gazebo_plugin nao_gazebo_plugin_H25.launch Wait ready, click Play button Gazebo. Finally, start MoveIt!: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch nao_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch based work Konstantinos Chatzilygeroudis: https://github.com/costashatz/nao_dcm. name: pepper_moveit_config
description: An automatically generated package with all the configuration and launch files for using the Pepper robot with the MoveIt Motion Planning Framework
readme: pepper_moveit_config MoveIt! config package generated MoveIt! wizard. requires Pepper model https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_robot/tree/master/pepper_description binary package ros-indigo-pepper-description moveit package run remote computer robot. Compile package pepper_moveit_config package compilation running rospack profile ready play Pepper Run MoveIt robot run moveit package unconnected robot attached robot (real simulated): standalone execution {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_moveit_config demo.launch launching demo, robot MoveIt! GUI ![image](tuto/moveit_launch.png){width="100.0%"} Check box Approximate Solutions optionally, choose Planning library), ready control robot. real robot MoveIt! real robot, instanciate ROS controllers robot. [pepper\_dcm\_bringup](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_dcm_robot) [pepper\_control](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_virtual) set NAO\_IP environment variable robot\'s address: {.sourceCode .bash} export NAO_IP=<YOUR_ROBOT_IP> Launch DCM bringup: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_dcm_bringup pepper_bringup.launch Wait ready, run: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch visual data robot's cameras integrate visual data robot's cameras MoveIt obstacle detection. ways acquire visual data: naoqi_driver {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch naoqi_driver naoqi_driver.launch pepper\_sensors\_py data single camera) {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_sensors_py camera.launch obstacle detection based Octomap library integrated MoveIt, change active camera depth camera rqt\_reconfigure. tune acquisition parameters needed: {.sourceCode .bash} rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure depth data configure Octomap represents robot\'s space set occupied free voxels. ![image](tuto/moveit_octomap.png){width="100.0%"} simulated robot Gazebo) MoveIt! Gazebo simulation, [pepper\_dcm\_bringup](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_dcm_robot) [pepper\_control](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_virtual) [pepper\_gazebo\_plugin](https://github.com/ros-naoqi/pepper_virtual) Launch Gazebo, {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_gazebo_plugin pepper_gazebo_plugin_Y20.launch Wait ready, click Play button Gazebo. Finally, start MoveIt!: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch pepper_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch Moveit: MoveIt GUI, check box approximate Solutions" bottom left control panel. Planning tab. Select part robot move: plugin list, MotionPlanning/Planning Request/Planning Group choose roobot's part control. interactive marker chosen robot's part. control robot drag dropping interactive markers. compute trajectory reach position, clicking 'Planning' button Planning tab. satisfied planned trajectory, real robot 'Execute' and/or 'Plan execute' buttons. NOTE: start state updated automatically, 'Select Start State' select 'Current' click 'Update'.

name: pioneer_bringup
description: pioneer_bringup provides roslaunch scripts for starting the core functionnalities of Adept MobileRobots Pioneer and Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer 2, Pioneer 3, Pioneer LX, AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur and Seekur Jr.)
readme: Pionner_bringup ROS package providing roslaunch functionalities starting Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur Compatible RosAria P2OS hardware controllers. Installation Depending install dependencies: ROSARIA P2OS Urg_node: Hokuyo lidars LMS1XX: Sick LMS100 lidar Sicktoolbox_wrapper: Sick LMS2xx lidar Sick_scan: Sick lidars list bellow) Usbcam: usb cameras webcams Freenect: Kinect Pionner_dashboard: GUI dashboard window Usage starting robot default parameters: roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch activate features arguments: Argument values Default Description controller rosaria, p2os rosaria Hardware driver controller controller_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyS0 Serial port communicate controller sonar true, false false Activate sonars laser hokuyo, lms_1xx, lms_2xx, lms_1xxx, lms_4xxx, lms_5xx, mrs_1xxx, mrs_6xxx, rms_3xx, tim_5xx, tim_7xx, tim_7xxS false Laser reference code ‘false’ laser usbcam true, false false launch usbcam node kinect true, false false launch kinect nodes dashboard true, false false launch GUI window rqt visualize sensors readings control motors roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch controller:=p2os laser:=lms_5xx usbcam:=true controls Laser node: arguments lidar parameters: Argument values Default Description laser_hostname Address Laser address hostname applicable) laser_serial_port /dev/ttySx /dev/ttyUSBx /dev/ttyUSB0 Laser Port applicable) laser_baselink_distance roll pitch yaw Distance baselink lidar meters setting broadcast) roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch laser:=lms_1xx laser_hostname:=192.168.0.4 laser_baselink_distance:='0.20 controls Camera node: arguments customize camera parameters: Argument Values Default Description camera_port /dev/videox device number) /dev/video0 USB Camera port camera_display true, false true Display video feed screen stop parameters: customize parameters config/camera.yaml file, image_width, image_height, pixel_format, camera_frame_id io_method. roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch usbcam:=true camera_display:=false camera_port:=/dev/video2 controls Kinect node: arguments customize kinect parameters: Argument Values Default Description kinect_data_skip Number frames skip kinect data kinect_id kinect device number roslaunch pioneer_bringup pioneer_bringup.launch kinect:=true kinect_data_skip:=5 issues open /dev/ttyUSB0: Permission denied frequent issue USB-Serial adapter connect robot hardware lidar. Basically give correct autorizations: sudo chmod /dev/ttyUSB0 issues report problem issues section

name: pioneer_teleop
description: The pioneer_teleop package provides teleoperation using keyboard, sockets or command line for the Adept MobileRobots Pioneer and Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer 2, Pioneer 3, Pioneer LX, AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur and Seekur Jr.).
readme: Pionner_teleop ROS package providing scripts teleoperation keyboard, sockets command line. package compatible robot ROS ecosystem, originally implemented Adept MobileRobots Pioneer Pioneer-compatible robots (Including Pioneer Pioneer Pioneer AmigoBot, PeopleBot, PatrolBot, PowerBot, Seekur Seekur case robot, read robot Pionner-compatible?” section Note: package tested ROS Kinetic Kame Installation Dependencies install pioneer_bringup package, https://github.com/amine_horseman/pionner_bringup.git Source Download clone repository caktin workspace: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/amineHorseman/pioneer_teleop.git rosdep install pioneer_teleop Build catkin packages source ~/catkin_ws catkin_make python scripts executable ~/catkin_ws/src/pioneer_teleop/nodes sudo chmod Usage ##Teleop Modes package, ways teleoperation: Keyboard teleoperation: roslaunch pioneer_teleop keyboard_teleop.launch control robot motors keyboard arrows, increase decrease speed, stop version keyboard teleoperation command: roslaunch pioneer_teleop discrete_keyboard_teleop.launch case, robot moves small period time seconds default) stops. Sockets teleoperation: Controls robot remotely throught socket commands move robot web interface internet/LAN). roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch expected commands “left” default, script listens port robot moves seconds. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _port:=12345 _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 Command line teleoperation move robot command line throught terminal ssh roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_commandline.launch _direction:=forward expected commands (_direction argument) “left” default, robot moves seconds speed. change parameters, extra arguments mentionned bellow: roslaunch pionner_teleop socket_teleop.launch _direction:=backward _speed:=0.3 _move_time:=2.0 robot Pionner-compatible? package compatible robot ROS long velocity commands published /cmd_vel topic section). modify .launch scripts remove pionner_bringup call, execute python scripts located /nodes folder. issues Velocity command topic default, scripts publish velocity commands /cmd_vel topic. case velocity commands topic Pionner-compatible robots, remap velocity topic /cmd_vel change topic python scripts /nodes folder issues report problem issues panel TODO Feel free contribute repository Test package Ros Kinetic Kame Add joystick_teleop mode Add xbox360_teleop mode Add web page socket_teleop

Compile package romeo_moveit_config package compilation, running rospack profile dcm packages compile nodes. order compile romeo_dcm packages, set environment variable AL_DIR path NAOqiSDK-c++ computer. run usual {.sourceCode .bash} catkin_make ready play romeo Run MoveIt robot run moveit package unconnected robot attached robot (real simulated): standalone execution {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_moveit_config demo.launch launching demo, Romeo MoveIt! GUI {width=”100.0%”} real ROMEO MoveIt! real robot, instanciate ROS controllers Romeo. romeo_dcm_bringup (https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_dcm_robot) romeo_control (https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_virtual) set NAO_IP environment variable robot's address: {.sourceCode .bash} export NAO_IP= Launch DCM bringup: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_dcm_bringup romeo_dcm_bringup_remote.launch Wait ready, run: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch simulated robot Gazebo) MoveIt! Gazebo simulation, romeo\_dcm\_bringup (<https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_dcm_robot>) romeo\_control (<https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_virtual>) romeo\_gazebo\_plugin (<https://github.com/ros-aldebaran/romeo_virtual>) Launch Gazebo, {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_gazebo_plugin romeo_gazebo_plugin_H37.launch Wait ready, click Play button Gazebo. Finally, start MoveIt!: {.sourceCode .bash} roslaunch romeo_moveit_config moveit_planner.launch Moveit: RVIZ open: MotionPlanning plugin launched. check box approximate Solutions" bottom left control panel. Planning tab. name: rtt_roscomm
description:
rtt_roscomm provides the necessary template files and cmake macros for
    automatic rtt typekit and transport generation from ROS msg files
readme: RTT ROS Communications Nomenclature Warning: “ROS Service” remote procedure call hapens ROS communication protocols “Orocos/RTT Service” grouping properties, functions, data ports. “ROS Services” satisfy similar role “Orocos/RTT Operations”. Contents package serves purposes. Orocos RTT Service publishing subscribing ROS topics Orocos RTT Services calling serving ROS services Orocos typekits ROS message primitive types template generating wrapper packages ROS .msg .srv files typekits .msg files transport plugin .msg files ros service proxy factories .srv files Plugins ROS Topics package global RTT service creating real-time-safe connections ROS topics Orocos RTT data ports. package Orocos connection policies: buffered unbuffered connections ROS topics. Publishing subscribing command, topic type inferred Orocos port type. Connection policies created operations: rostopic.connection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates connection buffer length rostopic.bufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME, BUFFER_LENGTH): Creates connection user-supplied buffer length. rostopic.unbufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates unbuffered connection, writing thread immediately publishs message (publishing real-time safe. Note TOPIC_NAME prefixed tilde resolved process’s private namespace, similarly topic names resolved rospy. ROS Services package global RTT service task-scoped service facilitating communication ROS services. global service, rosservice_registry register factories creating proxies ROS service clients servers. task-scoped service ued bind RTT operations operation callers ROS services. general, users task-scoped RTT service, similarly rtt_rosparam service task-scoped RTT service rosservice operations: rosservice.connect(RTT_OPERATION_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_TYPE) Connect RTT operation ROS service. Note function RTT operation operation operation caller. RTT_OPERATION_NAME: task-scoped operation/operation caller provided/required services separated dots foo.bar.baz.op) ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) ROS_SERVICE_TYPE: full typename service std_srvs/Empty) rosservice.disconnect(ROS_SERVICE_NAME) Disconnects RTT operation operation caller ROS service server client.. ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) rosservice.disconnectAll() Disconnects RTT operations operation callers ROS service servers clients. global RTT service rosservice_registry operations: rosservice_registry.registerServiceFactory(FACTORY): Register ROS service factory rosservice_registry.hasServiceFactory(TYPENAME): Check ROS service type registered rosservice_registry.geServiceFactory(TYPENAME): ROS service client/server factory Code Generation package facilities generating typekits ROS service types defined .srv files generating plugins register ROS service types rosservice_registry service. Usage Connecting Orocos Port ROS Topic Imports import("rtt_roscomm") Publish stream("my_component.my_output", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_output")) Subscribe stream("my_component.my_input", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_input")) set connections code: #include <rtt_roscomm/rtt_rostopic.h> Add port stream ROS topic this->ports()->addPort("my_port", my_port_); my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("my_ros_topic")); create privately-scoped component-scoped topic, Privately-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~my_private_ros_topic")); Component-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/COMPONENT_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~" this->getName() "/my_component_scoped_ros_topic")); Connecting RTT Operations ROS Services connect Orocos operation ROS service .ops script Orocos DeploymentComponent: Imports import("rtt_roscomm") import("rtt_std_srvs") Load application-specific component loadComponent("some_component_name","some_component_package::SomeComponent") Load rosservice RTT service components loadService("some_component_name","rosservice") Expose operation component ROS service some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_provided_service.some_operation", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Expose ROS service component some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_Required_service.some_operation_caller", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Making Package’s ROS .msg .srv Types Generally, create catkin package simply create_rtt_msgs script running: rosrun rtt_roscomm create_rtt_msgs my_msgs create package CMakeLists.txt package.xml: project(rtt_my_msgs) find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS rtt_roscomm) Generate typekits ros .msg files ros_generate_rtt_typekit(my_msgs) Generate plugin services my_msgs ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies(my_msgs) Call orocos_generate_package() export proper targets orocos_generate_package( DEPENDS my_msgs DEPENDS_TARGETS rtt_roscomm ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies() cmake function generate RTT plugin registers factories services named package plugin loaded. Design ROS Services rosservice_registry RTT service list ROS service clients servers RTT operations operationcallers, rosservice.connect operation, inspects argument Operation OperationCaller. RTT Operation, instantiate ROS service server wrapped RTT OperationCaller call operation. readme: RTT ROSParam package RTT service service-requester associating RTT component properties ROS parameters. Design RTT service enables setting/getting individual RTT properties RTT component to/from ROS parameter server, setting/getting properties component simultaneously. operations map operations roscpp library, store retrieved values RTT properties component. service requires RTT properties component match names ROS parameters, modulo namespace resolution policy Usage Service Interface Constants resolution policies): RELATIVE Relative resolution: ABSOLUTE Absolute resolution: PRIVATE Private resolution: COMPONENT Component resolution: “~COMPONENT_NAME/name” Operations (getting/setting params) Operations properties getAll() getAllComponentPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server COMPONENT namespace. getAllRelative() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server relative namespace. getAllAbsolute() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server absolute namespace. getAllPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server node’s private namespace. Operations setting properties setAll() setAllComponentPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property COMPONENT’s private namespace. setAllRelative() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property relative namespace. setAllAbsolute() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property absolute namespace. setAllPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property node’s private namespace. Operations single properties getParam(ros_name, rtt_name) ROS param ros_name store RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. get(name ,policy) Attempt property named populates properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. getRelative(name) getAbsolute(name) getPrivate(name) getComponentPrivate(name) Operations setting single properties setParam(ros_name, rtt_name) Set ROS param ros_name RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. set(name, policy) Attempt set property named stores properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. setRelative(name) setAbsolute(name) setPrivate(name) setComponentPrivate(name) Scripting Interface Components meant ROS parameters properties set ROS parameter server scripting interface: Import packages import("rtt_ros") ros.import("rtt_rosparam") Create components laodService("my_component","rosparam") parameters component namespace "~my_component/..." my_component.rosparam.getAll() "robot_description" property absolute namespace "/robot_description" my_component.rosparam.getAbsolute("robot_description") Alternatively: my_component.rosparam.get("robot_description",my_component.rosparam.ABSOLUTE) Interface component designed ROS parameters, easily access rosparam service RTT ServiceRequester. simple component “robot_description” ROS param global namespace (“/robot_description”) “publish_period” ROS param node’s private namespace (“~publish_period”) Include service requester interface #include <rtt_rosparam/rosparam.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: Storage RTT properties std::string robot_description_; double publish_period_; public: MyComponent::MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name) Add properties this->addProperty("robot_description",robot_description_); this->addProperty("publish_period",publish_period_); bool configureHook() bool all_params_found true; rosparam service requester boost::shared_ptr<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam> rosparam this->getProvider<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam>("rosparam"); parameters if(rosparam) ROS parameter "/robot_description" all_params_found rosparam->getAbsolute("robot_description"); ROS parameter "~publish_period" all_params_found rosparam->getPrivate("publish_priod"); return all_params_found; API documentation information.

name: rtt_tf
description: This package contains the components of the rtt_tf package
readme: RTT package Orocos RTT component utilizing rigid body transform library Orocos. component service operations requesting broadcasting sing batch transforms. Usage general, process single component. component operations bare interface RTT service. “lookupTransform” Lookup transform source target. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “lookupTransformAtTime” Lookup transform source target specific time. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “common_time”, ros::Time common time transform computed “broadcastTransform” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transform”, geometry\_msgs::TransformStamped “broadcastTransforms” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transforms”, std::vector<geometry_msgs::TransformStamped> operations, instantiate rtt_tf::RTT_TF component, connect components intend import("rtt_ros"); Import load component ros.import("rtt_tf"); loadComponent("tf","rtt_tf::RTT_TF"); Connect operations component's service component's required service (easily created API shown section) loadComponent("my_component","MyComponent"); connectServices("my_component","tf"); API rtt_tf/tf_interface.h header includes rtt_tf::TFInterface class adds required service RTT component. easily connecting component. create RTT service requester #include <rtt_tf/tf_interface.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: rtt_tf::TFInterface public: MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name), Add services tf_(this) bool startHook() ready connected return tf_.ready(); void updateHook() Lookup transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped tform tf_.lookupTransform("/world","/my_frame");

name: rviz_visual_tools
description: Helper functions for displaying and debugging data in Rviz via published markers
readme: Rviz Visual Tools API wrapper displaying shapes meshes Rviz helper functions publish markers. displaying debugging data. advanced robot visualization features, moveit_visual_tools builds class, ompl_visual_tools OMPL ROS user. package includes: Easy helper functions visualizing Rviz fast Basic geometric markers Rviz complex geometric shapes coordinate frames, framed boxes, planes, paths, graphs Ability quickly choose standard colors sizes Tools ensure proper connection Rviz publishing visualizations Shortcuts convert types points poses ROS msgs, Eigen, Batch publishing capabilities reduce throttling ROS messages publishing helper class Developed Dave Coleman Correll Robotics Lab, University Colorado Boulder Andy McEvoy Travis ROS Buildfarm Trusty Devel Source Build ROS Buildfarm AMD64 Trusty Debian Build Install Ubuntu Debian sudo apt-get install ros-jade-rviz-visual-tools Build Source Clone repository catkin workspace, rosdep install tool automatically download dependencies. Depending current version ROS, rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro jade Quick Start Demo random shapes generated Rviz, launch Rviz: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo_rviz.launch start demo: roslaunch rviz_visual_tools demo.launch Code API Doxygen documentation Usage assume helper functions class. functions assume publishing transforms world frame call /odom). Initialize Add includes: #include <rviz_visual_tools/rviz_visual_tools.h> Add class’s member variables: visualizing rviz rviz_visual_tools::RvizVisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; class’ constructor add: visual_tools_.reset(new rviz_visual_tools::RvizVisualTools("base_frame","/rviz_visual_markers")); Change parameter robot’s base frame, parameter you’d Rviz marker ROS topic. Tools code easily debug code visual markers Rviz Start rviz create marker ‘Add’ button bottom Choose marker topic topic constructor. Code snippet create pose xyz rotate pose degrees axis. publish pose arrow visualziation Rviz. Rviz fixed frame chosen code. Create pose Eigen::Affine3d pose; pose Eigen::AngleAxisd(M_PI/4, Eigen::Vector3d::UnitY()); rotate axis degrees pose.translation() Eigen::Vector3d( translate x,y,z Publish arrow vector pose ROS_INFO_STREAM_NAMED("test","Publishing Arrow"); visual_tools_->publishArrow(pose, rviz_visual_tools::RED, rviz_visual_tools::LARGE); Basic Publishing Functions visual_tools.h details documentation functions: publishSphere publishSpheres publishArrow/publishXArrow publishYArrow publishZArrow publishCuboid publishCone publishXYPlane publishXZPlane publishYZPlane publishLine publishPath publishPolygon publishBlock publishWireframeCuboid publishWireframeRectangle publishAxis publishAxisLabeled publishCylinder publishMesh publishMesh publishText publishTest Helper Functions Reset function deleteAllMarkers tells Rviz clear current markers displayed. Batch publish markers published prevent buffer overflow ROS messages. enableBatchPublishing() triggerBatchPublish() triggerBatchPublishAndDisable() Conversion functions convertPose convertPoint32ToPose convertPoseToPoint convertPoint convertPoint32 convertFromXYZRPY convertToXYZRPY Convenience functions generateRandomPose generateEmptyPose dRand fRand iRand getCenterPoint getVectorBetweenPoints Colors package helps quickly choose colors feel free send PRs colors needed BLACK, BLUE, BROWN, CYAN, DARK_GREY, GREEN, GREY, LIME_GREEN, MAGENTA, ORANGE, PINK, PURPLE, RED, WHITE, YELLOW, TRANSLUCENT_LIGHT, TRANSLUCENT, TRANSLUCENT_DARK, RAND, CLEAR, DEFAULT 'do change default color' Marker Sizes XXSMALL, XSMALL, SMALL, REGULAR, LARGE, xLARGE, xxLARGE, xxxLARGE, XLARGE, XXLARGE Batch Publishing feature markers saved array trigger recieved send markers Rviz visualization. markers published overflow Rviz message buffers. enable, enableBatchPublishing(true) trigger ``triggerBatchPublish()`. Visual Tools tool lets easily debug Eigen transforms Rviz. Demo rviz_visual_tools::TFVisualTools tf_visualizer; Eigen::Affine3d world_to_shelf_transform Eigen::Affine3d::Identity(); tf_visualizer.publishTransform(world_to_shelf_transform, "world", "shelf"); Testing Linting run roslint, command catkin-tools: catkin build --no-status --no-deps --make-args roslint run catkin lint, command catkin-tools: catkin lint command catkin-tools run small amount tests: catkin run_tests --no-deps Docker Image build docker image repo: docker build davetcoleman/rviz_visual_tools:jade run GUI: safest compromise access control server host xhost +local:root lazy reckless docker run --env="DISPLAY" --env="QT_X11_NO_MITSHM=1" --volume="/tmp/.X11-unix:/tmp/.X11-unix:rw" davetcoleman/rviz_visual_tools:jade export containerId=$(docker Close security hole: xhost -local:root Contribute send PRs helper functions, fixes,

Connect USBtoRS232 premissions set properly. /dev/ttyUSB0 similar crw-rw-XX- sudo chmod a+rw /dev/ttyUSB0 laser configured properly, run ros master roscore Run node follows:(make source workspace) rosrun sicktoolbox_wrapper sicklms _port:=/dev/ttyUSB0 _baud:=38400 RVIZ visualize simply rostopic suffice confirm working rosrun rviz rviz

name: tiny_slam
description: TinySLAM ROS implementation
readme: TinySLAM implementation ROS Description package ROS implentation tinySLAM (https://openslam.org/tinyslam.html) simpliest lightweight SLAM methods. Current implementation compatible ROS Jade adds improvements original method: alternative model grid map cell track average stored effective values (probability occupied scan’s quality account); option compute probability cell occupied based laser beam splits cell original implementation constant Hardware Requirements Current implementation supposes robot odometry data laser scan measurements horizontally mounted fixed laser scanner. data ROS topics Subscribed topics). launch-file run configure tinySLAM node: roslaunch tiny_slam tinyslam_run.launch path:=[path dataset] Dataset BAG format (http://wiki.ros.org/rosbag). comment rosbag node launch file real-time data replace dataset player. Nodes tiny_slam tiny_slam node takes sensor_msgs/LaserScan messages odometry data topic builds map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) retrieved ROS topic /map. Subscribed Topics (tf/tfMessage) Transforms relate frames laser, base, odometry laser_scan (sensor_msgs/LaserScan) Laser scans create map Published Topics map (nav_msgs/OccupancyGrid) map data topic (updated periodically) Parameters ~cell_type (string, default: “avg”) Accepted values: “avg” modified cell model; “base” original cell model ~occupancy_estimator (string, default: “const”) Accepted values: “const” original occupancy estimator; “area” dynamic occupancy estimator ~skip_exceeding_lsr_vals (boolean, default: false) Determines handle range laser measurements: true ignore measurements, false process empty cells. ~base_occupied_prob (float, default: initial probability cell occupied probability laser scan data valid) ~base_empty_prob (float, default: initial probability cell empty probability range laser scan data valid) Required Transforms base_link odom_combined: odometry system driver mobile base) Transforms map robot_pose: current estimate robot’s pose map frame Contributors Artur Huletski Dmitriy Kartashov Kirill Krinkin Copyright JetBrains Research, Mobile Robot Algorithms Laboratory

name: youbot_driver
description: driver for the KUKA youBot robot
readme: youBot API API documentation basic idea youBot API represent robot system combination decoupled functional sub-systems. API represents manipulator arm base platform combination joints. time joint defined combination motor gearbox. main classes youBot API user aware YouBotManipulator class represents youBot arm aggregation joints gripper YouBotBase class represents youBot base platform YouBotJoint class represents joints manipulator base Installation System requirements: Linux Ethernet adapter Root access Ethernet adapter libraries required youBot API: Simple Open EtherCAT master (included driver) Boost Libraries Installation rosmake: Install minimal installation ROS. ros.org) Clone youBot API sources: git clone git://github.com/youbot/youbot_driver.git Add repository folder ROS_PACKAGE_PATH environment variable. Compile youbot driver typing: rosmake youbot_driver --rosdep-install Manual installation: gnu-make software system (version required), working compiler chain. console commands exemplary Ubuntu Linux. git version control software: sudo apt-get install git git-core Cmake cross platform sudo apt-get install cmake download youBot API sources type: git clone git://github.com/youbot/youbot_driver.git Install boost. sudo apt-get install libboost-all-dev able compile youBot API. <your_folder>/youbot_driver mkdir build build cmake library generated folder ~/youbot_driver/lib. Usage youBot API give complete joint level access youBot joints. youBot joint represented youbot::YouBotJoint class API. stage difference base joint powers wheel manipulator joint. classes youbot::YouBotBase youbot::YouBotManipulator access youbot::YouBotJoint instance joint. set setpoint read sensor values form joints youbot::JointData classes. instance youbot::JointVelocitySetpoint youbot::JointSensedCurrent. configure parameters joint, youbot::JointParameter classes. instance youbot::MaximumPositioningVelocity. Programs find programs youbot_applications repository. Documentation detailed information class methods refer API documentation. generate documentation source code type: sudo apt-get install doxygen build doc Run sudo youBot Driver access raw ethernet device. Linux normal user access raw ethernet device. grand capability program tool setcap. install setcap sudo apt-get install libcap2-bin provide program raw access ethernet device (replace ./YouBot_KeyboardRemoteControl program.) sudo setcap cap_net_raw+ep ./YouBot_KeyboardRemoteControl executable created replaces building. License software published dual-license: GNU Lesser General Public License LGPL BSD license. dual-license implies users code choose terms prefer.

name: euslisp
description:
EusLisp is an integrated programming system for the
  research on intelligent robots based on Common Lisp and
  Object-Oriented programming
readme: EusLisp EusLisp originally developed Toshihiro Matusi AIST page repository maintained JSK University Tokyo. Introduction EusLisp integrated programming system research intelligent robots based Common Lisp Object-Oriented programming. principal subjects field robotics research sensory data processing, visual environment recognition, collision avoiding motion planning, task planning. problem, dimensional shape models robots environment play crucial roles. motivation development EusLisp demand extensible solid modeler easily higher level symbolic processing system. Investigations traditional solid modelers proved vital requirement implementation language list processing capability represent manage topology model components. Numerical computation power important, locality geometric computation suggested provision vector/matrix functions built-ins greatly ease programming. EusLisp Manual, Part EusLisp Basics, Introduction) Easy Installation Demo Program check http://jskeus.sourceforge.net easy installation current demo programs. programs euslisp/eus/lib/ euslisp/eus/contrib/ directories out-dated. Download Compile Note: strongly recommend check http://github.com/euslisp/jskeus download compile euslisp irteus; humanoid model description function layer euslisp description euslisp hard-core developers. git clone https://github.com/euslisp/EusLisp euslisp euslisp export ARCHDIR=Linux64 export EUSDIR=`pwd` export PATH=$PATH:$EUSDIR/$ARCHDIR/bin export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=$LD_LIBRARY_PATH:$EUSDIR/$ARCHDIR/lib lisp Makefile.Linux64 Makefile eus0 eus1 eus2 eusg eusx eusgl eus cygwin sytem, set ARCHDIR=Cygwin Makefile.Cygwin Makefile 32bit Linux system, set ARCHDIR=Linux Makefile.Linux.thread Makefile Documnets online manual. (Japanese translation manual) Download PDF version (English, Japanese) Original EusLisp Homepage Check original EusLisp homepage EusLisp: Object-Oriented Concurrent Lisp Solid Modeling Facilities Robots EusLisp based Robot Software Jijo-2 Robot Remote brain robots (apelike, hanzo, sasuke, igoid, akira, haru, gibbon, jikokuten, taro, kaz Musculoskeletal humanoids: Kotaro, Kojiro Robot Platform JSK: Aibo, Hoap, HRP2W, HRP2JSK, Nao

name: jskeus
description: EusLisp software developed and used by JSK at The University of Tokyo
readme: JSK EusLisp repository repository containts software devleoped JSK University Tokyo. started Install binary (RECOMMENDED: Ubuntu Setup ROS repository sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu `lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' wget http://packages.ros.org/ros.key sudo apt-key add echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc sudo apt-get update Install Euslisp sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-euslisp Install Homebrew (OSX) brew install euslisp/jskeus/jskeus Playing Demo programs Motion generation irteusgl irteus/demo/demo.l type command, ‘(crank-motion)’ ‘(dual-arm-ik)’ Robots objects models irteusgl models/irt-all-robots.l "(make-all-robots)" irteusgl models/irt-all-objects.l "(make-all-objects)" started ROS users Install binaries (RECOMMENDED: Ubuntu Setup ROS repository skip procdeure setup ROS system sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu `lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' wget http://packages.ros.org/ros.key sudo apt-key add echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc sudo apt-get update Install pr2/euslisp programs sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-pr2eus Running demo programs Visualizinot robot rosrun euscollada pr2.sh real users set ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP robot simulated command roscd pr2eus read joint state display euslisp viewer, print jacobian roslaunch ./pr2-read-state.launch sample program show posture euslisp, MOVE REAL ROBOT, CAUTION!!! roslaunch ./pr2-send-joints.launch script set ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP source `rospack find jsk_tools`/src/bashrc.ros rossetrobot pr1012.jsk.com change environment rossetip roseus package http://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg repository ROS client library euslisp Support Trouble Shooting issue tracker support pull requests report bugs patches. Online Documents Japanese avilable) online manual PDF files Install euslisp source code (EXPERTS Installing dependent libraries Ubuntu users sudo apt-get install git gcc libjpeg-dev libxext-dev libx11-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglu1-mesa-dev libpq-dev libpng12-dev xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi gsfonts-x11 texlive-fonts-extra xfonts-100dpi-transcoded xfonts-75dpi-transcoded msttcorefonts Mac OSX users Homebrew brew install jpeg libpng mesalib-glw wget ’'’NOTE:’’’ EusLisp Library, Mac OSX mountain lion longer support install substitution XQuartz. Mac OSX lion older) users install latest XQuartz safety. XQuartz older installation EusLisp fail. install latest XQuartz.) Raspberry users debian(Raspbian “wheezy”) sudo apt-get install git gcc libjpeg62-dev libxext-dev libx11-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglu1-mesa-dev libpq-dev libpng12-dev xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi additional install OpenGL raspberry sudo apt-get install libgl1-mesa-dri xserver-xorg-video-vesa xutils Cygwin(now working 32bit Packages listed intalled setup.exe. git, gcc-core, gcc-g++, libjpeg-devel, libpng-devel libGL-devel, libGLU-devel, libglut-devel, libX11-devel, libXext-devel xorg-server X-start-menu-icons startxwin.exe) mesa-demo glxinfo) bitmap fonts bitmap font packages -75dpi, -100dpi, -misc Downloading building EusLisp git clone http://github.com/euslisp/jskeus jskeus jskeus instructions add EUSDIR, ARCHDIR, PATH, LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable bashrc invoke euslisp program echo "source ~/bashrc.eus" ~/.bashrc Install ROS jsk-ros-pkg repository http://wiki.ros.org/indigo/Installation/Source detail sudo apt-get install python-rosdep python-rosinstall-generator python-wstool python-rosinstall build-essential mkdir ~/ros_catkin_ws/src ~/ros_catkin_ws/src wstool init src https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_pr2eus/master/.rosinstall wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_travis/master/rosdep-install.sh ./rosdep-install.sh catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Acknowledgment software repository based EusLisp language.

name: libccd
description: libccd is library for collision detection between two convex shapes.
readme: libccd libccd library collision detection convex shapes. libccd implements variation Gilbert–Johnson–Keerthi algorithm Expand Polytope Algorithm (EPA) implements algorithm Minkowski Portal Refinement (MPR, a.k.a. XenoCollide) Game Programming Gems libccd open source library knowledge include MPR algorithm working space. library called mpr2d, implemented programming language, works space. libccd part ODE library ODE’s ./configure enable FCL library Willow Garage, Bullet3 library (https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3). implementation details GJK algorithm, http://www.win.tue.nl/~gino/solid/jgt98convex.pdf. Dependencies library based standard libraries. exception testsuites built top (https://github.com/danfis/cu) library licensed LGPL, testing depends libccd library distributed License libccd licensed OSI-approved 3-clause BSD License, text license distributed source code BSD-LICENSE file. file include license notice, rest considered licensed 3-clause BSD License. Compile Install libccd mechanisms compiling installing. simple Makefile, autotools, CMake. Makefile Directory src/ Makefile needed compilation installation: src/ install Library libccd default compiled double precision floating point numbers change options USE_SINGLE/USE_DOUBLE, USE_SINGLE=yes compile library single precision. Installation directory changed options PREFIX, INCLUDEDIR LIBDIR. info type Autotools libccd support autotools: Generate configure script ./bootstrap Create build/ directory: mkdir build build Run configure script: ../configure Run install: install configure script change libccd compiled installed, significant option –enable-double-precision enables double precision (single default case). name: razor_imu_9dof
description: razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver for the Sparkfun OpenLog Artemis, 9DoF Razor IMU M0, 9DOF Razor IMU and 9DOF Sensor Stick. It also provides Arduino firmware that runs on the board, and which must be installed on it for the system to work. A node which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the board (or any IMU) is provided for testing.
authors: Tang Tiong Yew, Kristof Robot, Paul Bouchier, Peter Bartz
readme: Official ROS Documentation extensive standard ROS-style version documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof Install Configure ROS Package Install dependencies visualization): sudo apt-get install python-visual Ubuntu Ubuntu install https://github.com/lebarsfa/visual/tree/bionic, https://github.com/ENSTABretagneRobotics/razor_imu_9dof/issues/47 sudo apt-get install python3-pip python3-wxgtk4.0 pip3 install vpython Ubuntu Download code: ~/catkin_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/ENSTABretagneRobotics/razor_imu_9dof.git catkin_make visualization, Ubuntu src/razor_imu_9dof/nodes wget https://www.glowscript.org/docs/VPythonDocs/VPtoGS.py python3 VPtoGS.py Converted/display_3D_visualization.py display_3D_visualization.py Install Arduino firmware SPX-15846 DEV-16832 (OpenLog Artemis), follow instructions OLA_IMU_Basics.ino sample https://github.com/sparkfun/OpenLog_Artemis drivers https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/how-to-install-ch340-drivers, install SparkFun Apollo3 boards Arduino IDE https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/installing-board-definitions-in-the-arduino-ide (add https://raw.githubusercontent.com/sparkfun/Arduino_Boards/master/IDE_Board_Manager/package_sparkfun_index.json File Preferences Additional Board Manager URLs) ensure select SparkFun Apollo3 SparkFun RedBoard Artemis ATP board install SparkFun ICM IMU Arduino library https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/installing-an-arduino-library). SEN-14001 (9DoF Razor IMU follow instructions default firmware https://learn.sparkfun.com/tutorials/9dof-razor-imu-m0-hookup-guide updated version SparkFun_MPU-9250-DMP_Arduino_Library https://github.com/lebarsfa/SparkFun_MPU-9250-DMP_Arduino_Library updated version default firmware https://github.com/lebarsfa/9DOF_Razor_IMU). Open src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino Arduino IDE. Note: modified version Peter Bartz’ original Arduino code https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs). version emits linear acceleration angular velocity data required ROS Imu message Select hardware uncommenting line src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino, HARDWARE OPTIONS Select hardware uncommenting line! //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10125" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10736" (HMC5883L magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DoF Razor IMU version "SEN-14001" //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "OpenLog Artemis" version "SPX-15846" #define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "OpenLog Artemis" version "DEV-16832" //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10183" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10321" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10724" (HMC5883L magnetometer) Upload Arduino sketch board Configure default configuration, razor_imu_9dof expects yaml config file my_razor.yaml USB port Calibration parameters examplerazor.yaml file Copy file my_razor.yaml roscd razor_imu_9dof/config razor.yaml my_razor.yaml edit my_razor.yaml needed Launch Publisher roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub.launch Publisher visualization: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-and-display.launch Publisher diagnostics: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-diags.launch visualization roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-display.launch Calibrate accuracy, follow tutorial calibrate sensors: http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof updated version Peter Bartz’s magnetometer calibration scripts https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs magnetometer_calibration directory. Update my_razor.yaml calibration parameters. Dynamic Reconfigure launched publisher launch commands listed dynamically reconfigure yaw calibration. Run: rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure Select imu_node. Change slider move calibration degrees. running visualization you’ll display jump calibration takes effect. intent feature tune alignment AHRS direction robot driving direction, determine AHRS reads degrees robot degrees shown GPS, tune calibration read compass-equivalent bore-sighting camera.

name: stereo_slam

readme: Stereo SLAM stereo_slam ROS node execute Simultaneous Localization Mapping (SLAM) stereo camera. algorithm designed tested underwater robotics. node based library graph optimization power libhaloc find loop closures graph nodes. keyframe multi-keyframe loop closing mechanism, based keypoint clustering, improve SLAM corrections feature-poor environments. action Specially designed underwater scenarios: Video: ORB-SLAM Stereo SLAM (underwater) videos… Video: Stereo SLAM reconstruction Video: Stereo SLAM UIB outdoor pond Installation (Ubuntu ROS Indigo) Install dependencies sudo apt-get install ros-<your ros distro>-libg2o sudo apt-get install libceres-dev setup stereo visual odometer viso2 fovis). Download code, save ROS workspace enviroment compile. roscd git clone https://github.com/srv/stereo_slam.git stereo_slam rosmake Parameters odom_topic Visual odometry topic (type nav_msgs::Odometry). camera_topic namespace stereo camera. (hard-coded) parameters include/constants.h set node parameters. Default parameters work. Run node run node launch file performance scale images width). <launch> <arg name="camera" default="/stereo"/> Run stereo image proc <node ns="$(arg camera)" pkg="stereo_image_proc" type="stereo_image_proc" name="stereo_image_proc" <node pkg="viso2_ros" type="stereo_odometer" name="stereo_odometer"> <remap from="stereo" to="$(arg camera)"/> <remap from="image" to="image_rect"/> </node> <node pkg="stereo_slam" type="localization" name="stereo_slam" output="screen"> <param name="odom_topic" value="/stereo_odometer/odometry"/> <param name="camera_topic" value="$(arg camera)"/> </node> </launch> Published Topics /stereo_slam/odometry vehicle pose (type nav_msgs::Odometry). /stereo_slam/graph_poses updated graph poses (type stereo_slam::GraphPoses). /stereo_slam/keyframes Number inserted keyframes (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/keypoints_clustering Image keypoint clusters (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings Image loop closing correspondences. Correspondences keyframe-to-multi-keyframe (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/loop_closing_queue Number keyframes waiting loop closing queue. monitor topic, check real-time performance: number grows indefinitely means system able process keyframes, scale images. (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/loop_closings Number loop closings (type std_msgs::String). /stereo_slam/pointcloud pointcloud keyframe (type sensor_msgs::PointCloud2). /stereo_slam/tracking_overlap Image representation traking overlap. decide insert keyframe graph (type sensor_msgs::Image). /stereo_slam/camera_params optimized (calibrated) camera parameters loop closure (type stereo_slam::CameraParams). Saved data node stores data stereo_slam directory execution: haloc folder files needed libhaloc library, responsible loop closing detection. folder keyframes Stores left stereo image keyframe possibility drawing keypoint clustering image). loop_closures Stores images published topic /stereo_slam/loop_closing_matchings. pointclouds Stores pointclouds published topic /stereo_slam/pointcloud. Online graph viewer node python script visualize results stereo_slam execution: scripts/graph_viewer.py: usage: graph_viewer.py ground_truth_file visual_odometry_file graph_vertices_file graph_edges_file (automatically detect graph files): roscd stereo_slam ./scripts/graph_viewer.py odometry file recorded ros rostopic echo /your_odometry_topic/odometry odometry.txt ground truth file format odometry. Post processing node python script evaluate results stereo_slam execution finishes: scripts/slam_evaluation.py: usage: slam_evaluation.py ground_truth_file visual_odometry_file graph_vertices_file graph_edges_file script perform set operations order evaluate performance stereo_slam algorithm: Align curves (ground truth, visual_odometry graph vertices) origin. Compute average translation error. Plot error trajectory distance.

name: vicon_bridge
description:
This is a driver providing data from VICON motion capture systems. It is based on the vicon_mocap package from the starmac stacks. 
     Additionally, it can handle multiple subjects / segments and allows to calibrate an origin of the vehicle(s) as this is somehow tedious with the VICON Tracker.
readme: driver providing data VICON motion capture systems. based vicon_mocap package starmac stacks. Additionally, handle multiple subjects segments calibrate origin vehicle(s) tedious VICON Tracker. License: BSD Author: Markus Achtelik <markus.achtelik@mavt.ethz.ch> QUICK START roslaunch vicon_bridge vicon.launch set "datastream_hostport" parameter vicon computer's ip/hostname OPERATION vicon_bridge node initiates connection Vicon data source Nexus Tracker) DataStream API. parameter ~datastream_hostport set address port (joined colon) DataStream server machine, default port). parameter ~stream_mode call DataStream SetStreamMode method; valid values "ServerPush", "ClientPull". subjects segments recognized automatically published transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped ORIGIN CALIBRATION object created vicon tracker, places origin arbitrary object. Setting desired origin Tracker tedious, added origin calibration method. Place vehicle vicon origin set vicon calibration, measure/estimate height offset) desired origin floor. run rosrun vicon_bridge calibrate <subject <segment offset> Poses grabbed, averaged vehicle vicon's origin. calibration parameters stored parameter server ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose startup, node checks calibration parameters time running rviz observe correct Published topics: vicon/<subject_name>/<segment_name> publishes subjects/segments vicon/markers publishes labeled unlabeled markers. Labeled markers affected origin calibration Services: grab_vicon_pose grab poses subject/segment averages calibrate_segment calls origin calibration segment transforms vicon/<subject_name>/<segment_name> Parameters stream_mode mode connect DataStream server. Values: "ClientPull", "ServerPush". Default: "ClientPull" datastream_hostport host:port DataStream server tf_ref_frame_id reference frame Default: "world" ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/w ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/x ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/y ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/orientation/z quaternion pose origin vehicle ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/position/x ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/position/y ~/<subject_name>/segment_name/zero_pose/position/z position pose origin vehicle

name: contact_handler
description:
The contact_handler package. It reports the dynamic-engine-reported contacts between
      all scene objects on a topic called "contacts".

      This package uses gazebo_msgs/ContactsState message type to report body contacts.
      This is because ROS doesn't have any standardized contact reporting messages.
      However, to have gazebo_msgs, you don't need to install whole Gazebo, you just need to
      install the single package ros-indigo-gazebo-msgs.
