name: flaky
description: Plugin for nose or py.test that automatically reruns flaky tests.
readme: flaky Flaky plugin nose py.test automatically reruns flaky tests. Ideally, tests reliably pass fail, test fixtures rely components reliable. flaky, removing tests marking \@skip, automatically retried. information flaky, presentation. Marking tests flaky mark test flaky, simply import flaky decorate test \@flaky: {.sourceCode .python} flaky import flaky {.sourceCode .python} @flaky def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') default, flaky retry failing test behavior overridden passing values flaky decorator. accepts parameters: max_runs, min_passes; flaky run tests max_runs times, succeeded min_passes times. test passes min_passes times, considered success; run max_runs times passing min_passes times, considered failure. {.sourceCode .python} @flaky(max_runs=3, min_passes=2) def test_something_that_usually_passes(self): test pass run times.””” value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double ‘Result doubled incorrectly.’) Marking class flaky addition marking single test flaky, entire test cases marked flaky: {.sourceCode .python} @flaky class TestMultipliers(TestCase): def test_flaky_doubler(self): value_to_double result get_result_from_flaky_doubler(value_to_double) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_double 'Result doubled incorrectly.') @flaky(max_runs=3) def test_flaky_tripler(self): value_to_triple result get_result_from_flaky_tripler(value_to_triple) self.assertEqual(result, value_to_triple 'Result tripled incorrectly.') \@flaky class decorator mark test_flaky_doubler flaky, override max_runs test_flaky_tripler decorator test method). rerun types failures Depending tests, failures due flakiness. rerunning failures, filter function flaky fail test {.sourceCode .python} def is_not_crash(err, *args): return issubclass(err[0], ProductCrashedError) @flaky def test_something(): raise ProductCrashedError @flaky(rerun_filter=is_not_crash) def test_something_else(): raise ProductCrashedError Flaky run [test\_something]{.title-ref} run [test\_something\_else]{.title-ref} incur delay test retries: {.sourceCode .python} import time def delay_rerun(*args): time.sleep(1) return True @flaky(rerun_filter=delay_rerun) def test_something_else(): Activating plugin nose plugin, flaky activated command line: {.sourceCode .console} nosetests –with-flaky py.test, flaky automatically run. disabled command line: {.sourceCode .console} py.test no:flaky Command line arguments Flaky Report Pass --no-flaky-report suppress report run detailing flaky test results. Shorter Flaky Report Pass --no-success-flaky-report suppress information successful flaky tests. Force Flaky Pass --force-flaky treat tests flaky. Pass --max-runs=MAX_RUNS and/or --min-passes=MIN_PASSES control behavior flaky --force-flaky Flaky decorators individual tests override defaults. Additional usage examples code test/test_example.py Installation install, simply: {.sourceCode .console} pip install flaky Compatibility Flaky tested test runners options: Nosetests. Doctests marked flaky. Py.test. Works [pytest-xdist]{.title-ref} [\--boxed]{.title-ref} option. Doctests marked flaky. Contributing [CONTRIBUTING.rst](https://github.com/box/flaky/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.rst). Setup Create virtual environment install packages {.sourceCode .console} mkvirtualenv flaky pip install requirements-dev.txt Testing Run tests {.sourceCode .console} tox tox tests include code style checks pep8 pylint. name: genty
description: Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands for "generate tests". It promotes generative testing, where a single test can execute over a variety of input.
readme: genty Genty, pronounced "gen-tee", stands "generate tests". promotes generative testing, single test execute variety input. Genty breeze. file sample.py code test tests: {.sourceCode .python} genty import genty, genty_repeat, genty_dataset unittest import TestCase Here’s class test class MyClass(object): def add_one(self, return Here’s test code @genty class MyClassTests(TestCase): @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) Running `MyClassTests` default unittest runner {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_add_one(0, (sample.MyClassTests) test_add_one(100000, (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests write multiple independent tests test cases, code refactored parametrized genty! produces readable tests. produces maintainable tests. produces expressive tests. option running test multiple times. stress tests exercising code race conditions. test {.sourceCode .python} @genty_repeat(3) def test_adding_one_to_zero(self): self.assertEqual(1, MyClass().add_one(0)) run times, producing output {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_adding_one() iteration_1 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_2 (sample.MyClassTests) test_adding_one() iteration_3 (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests techniques combined: {.sourceCode .python} @genty_repeat(2) @genty_dataset( def test_add_one(self, expected_result): actual_result MyClass().add_one(value) self.assertEqual(expected_result, actual_result) options explore including naming datasets `genty_args`. {.sourceCode .python} @genty_dataset( default_case=(0, limit_case=(999, error_case=genty_args(-1, is_something=False), def test_complex(self, optional_value=None, is_something=True): run tests, producing output {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_complex(default_case) (sample.MyClassTests) test_complex(limit_case) (sample.MyClassTests) test_complex(error_case) (sample.MyClassTests) Ran tests `@genty_datasets` chained semantically datasets keeping separate expressiveness. {.sourceCode .python} @genty_dataset(10, @genty_dataset('first', 'second') def test_composing(self, parameter_value): run tests, producing output {.sourceCode .console} python unittest sample test_composing(10) (sample.MyClassTests) test_composing(100) (sample.MyClassTests) test_composing(u’first’) (sample.MyClassTests) test_composing(u’second’) name: libhaloc
description: Hash-Based Loop Closing
readme: libhaloc ROS library HAsh-based LOop Closure detection. library tools loop closing detection based image hashing. Image hashing consists representing image small vector (hash). hash image compared hash image super fast order determine images similar. image hashing implemented library based SIFT features SIFT invariant scale rotation, image hashing invariant properties. library works mono stereo cameras transformation mono stereo) loop closures Related paper Autonomous Robots CITATION: @Article{Negre Carrasco2015, author="Negre Carrasco, Pep Lluis Bonin-Font, Francisco Oliver-Codina, Gabriel", title="Global image signature visual loop-closure detection", journal="Autonomous Robots", year="2015", pages="1--15", issn="1573-7527", doi="10.1007/s10514-015-9522-4", url="http://dx.doi.org/10.1007/s10514-015-9522-4" prepare SLAM node Modify CMakeLists.txt Add line catkin_package() find_package(libhaloc REQUIRED) Include libhaloc directories include_directories( ${catkin_INCLUDE_DIRS} ${libhaloc_INCLUDE_DIRS} Link executable libhaloc target_link_libraries(your_executable_here ${catkin_LIBRARIES} ${libhaloc_LIBRARIES} Include header .cpp file: #include <libhaloc/lc.h> call library libhaloc ways: generate image hashes techniques compare hashes retrieve candidates close loop. full power libhaloc search loop closing candidates compute homogeneous transformation libhaloc option: Declare haloc object: haloc::Hash haloc_; Init haloc object (!hash_.isInitialized()) hash_.init(c_cluster_.getSift()); image process, extract descriptor matrix (SIFT) cv::Mat sift; opencv extract SIFT matrix vector<float> current_hash hash_.getHash(sift); store hashes table compare std::vector<float> matches; (uint i<my_table.size(); float hash_.match(current_hash, my_table[i]); matches.push_back(matches); Finally, sort vector matches smallest largest. smallest values candidates close loop. libhaloc option: Declare haloc object object option haloc::LoopClosure haloc_; Set haloc parameters initialize haloc::LoopClosure::Params params; params.work_dir haloc_.setParams(params); haloc_.init(); image, call function setNode() store image properties getLoopClosure() loop closure image previous images. Mono version int loop_closure_with; index image closes loop inserted tf::Transform trans; transformation loop closure haloc_.setNode(img); bool valid haloc_.getLoopClosure(loop_closure_with, trans); Stereo version int loop_closure_with; index image closes loop inserted tf::Transform trans; transformation loop closure haloc_.setNode(img_left, img_right); bool valid haloc_.getLoopClosure(loop_closure_with, trans); cases, valid true, loop closure Check integration Stereo Slam. Important Parameters work_dir Directory library save image information writable!). desc_matching_type CROSSCHECK RATIO. desc_thresh_ratio Descriptor threshold crosscheck matching (typically ratio ratio matching (typically min_neighbor Minimum number neighbors skipped loop closure (typically depends frame rate). n_candidates candidates hash matching (typically min_matches Minimum number descriptor matches matching loop closure min_inliers Minimum number inliers matching loop closure

name: ompl_visual_tools
description: Rviz 3-D visualizer for planning algorithms implemented with the Open Motion Planning Library (OMPL)
readme: OMPL Visual Tools OMPL Visual Tools library visualizing debugging Open Motion Planning Library algorithms Rviz. Formally named ompl_rviz_viewer August project spun internship Willow Garage. Video ompl_visual_tools originally develped testing cost-based algorithms dimensional space dimension displayed cost. space grey scale cost map image passed program. lighter (closer white) pixel image “higher cost” considered Black considered cost. Additionally, absolute obstacles defining max limit cost, threshold considered obstacle. program similar OMPL.app distributed OMPL, RViz visualization streamlined costs experience-based planning. Developed Dave Coleman University Colorado Boulder Willow Garage Status: Travis Devel Job Status AMD64 Debian Job Status Install Ubuntu Debian sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-ompl-visual-tools Build Source build package, git clone repo catkin workspace install dependencies running command root catkin workspace: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Code API Class Reference Usage library integrated project easily view robot planning environment Rviz. load visualizer: visual tools interfacing Rviz ompl_visual_tools::OmplVisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; Load tool displaying Rviz visual_tools_.reset(new ompl_visual_tools::OmplVisualTools(BASE_FRAME)); visual_tools_->setSpaceInformation(si_); visual_tools_->setGlobalScale(100); Clear current rviz makers visual_tools_->deleteAllMarkers(); Dimensions optional cost map test environment cost map: Cost ompl::base::CostMap2DOptimizationObjectivePtr cost_map_; cost_map_->max_cost_threshold_percent_ max_cost_threshold_percent; cost_map_->loadImage(image_path); Pass cost visualizer visual_tools_->setCostMap(cost_map_->cost_); view cost map Rviz: visual_tools_->publishCostMap(cost_map_->image_); view start goal location: visual_tools_->publishState(start, rviz_visual_tools::GREEN, rviz_visual_tools::XLARGE, "plan_start_goal"); visual_tools_->publishState(goal, rviz_visual_tools::ORANGE, rviz_visual_tools::XLARGE, "plan_start_goal"); view solution path: Interpolate solution simple_setup_->getSolutionPath().interpolate(); Show path visual_tools_->publishPath( simple_setup_->getSolutionPath(), rviz_visual_tools::GREEN, "final_solution"); planner Visualize explored space visual_tools_->publishGraph(planner_data, rviz_visual_tools::ORANGE, "tree"); Visualize sample locations visual_tools_->publishSamples(planner_data); MoveIt! Robot Planning moveit_visual_tools information tools class MoveIt!. OMPL-specific features: set state space MoveIt! chosen robot OMPL: Create state space describing robot's planning group ompl_interface::ModelBasedStateSpaceSpecification model_ss_spec(moveit_robot_model, joint_model_group); const ompl_interface::JointModelStateSpaceFactory factory; ompl_interface::ModelBasedStateSpacePtr model_state_space factory.getNewStateSpace(model_ss_spec); Setup state space model_state_space->setup(); visual_tools_->setStateSpace(model_state_space); publish paths tips robot, planned OMPL: std::vector<ompl::base::PlannerDataPtr> paths; simple_setup.getAllPlannerDatas(paths); tip links setup std::vector<const robot_model::LinkModel*> tips; joint_model_group_->getEndEffectorTips(tips); bool show_trajectory_animated true; visual_tools_->publishRobotPath(paths[0], joint_model_group, tips, show_trajectory_animated); View Rviz Start Rviz included launch file: roslaunch ompl_visual_tools ompl_rviz.launch Demo planner standard RRT: rosrun ompl_visual_tools rrt_demo optional parameters, pass argument. options. Note: change algorithm manually edit code Cost Map Usage default cost map image located resources/ folder, running algorithm. Optionally pass cost map image command line argument, shown rosrun ompl_visual_tools rrt_demo cost_map.ppm image PPM “Netpbm color image” format. convert jpg, png image format Linux simply “convert” command, shown sudo apt-get install convert convert cost_map.png cost_map.ppm general, pixel image decent space size, larger dimensions require computational resources hang computer. name: openreroc_motion_sensor
description: This package supports a ultra sonic sensor using an FPGA board (ZedBoard Xilinx).
readme: openreroc_motion_sensor OpenReroc (Open Reconfigurable Robot Component) project build open source platform reconfigurable FPGA) devices robot components. package supports ultra sonic sensor FPGA board (ZedBoard Xilinx). openreroc_motion_sensor publishes sensor data topic. WARNING: machines) run work, timing problems roscore. restart experiment time, run roscore starting experiment. maps ‘maps’ folder. map patrol graph visible file maps/X/X-graph.png algorithms implemented ‘src’ folder. method implemented class ‘X_Agent’ inherits abstract class ‘PatrolAgent’ common services functions. Results experiments stored ‘results’ folder. order run experiment set experiments, run-exp.sh script template. convenient copy file file edit current version run_exp.sh run experiment DISlabs, robots, minutes, DTAP algorithm, standard parameters. minutes experiment terminates results files results/{map}_{n.robots}/{algorithm}/{machine}/{date}* result files produced: info.csv summary results experiment CSV format values: Map robots Goal wait time Communication delay Navigation module MRP Algorithm MRP Algorithm parameters Machine Date Sim Time Real time Interferences Termination status Idleness min avg stddev max Interference rate Total visits Avg visit node Complete patrol cycles results.txt information evolution results text format idleness.csv results CSV format Time Robot Node Node Idleness Interferences timeresults.csv evolution time results CSV format Time Idleness min avg stddev max Interferences script extended run multiple experiments single session, adding commands examples (possibly parameters). NAVIGATION MODULES Default navigation modules standard ROS nodes amcl (localization) move_base (path planning motion control). work fine, require computational resources limit simulation robots single machine. navigation modules, called spqrel_navigation, https://github.com/LCAS/spqrel_navigation/wiki. localizer planner interface amcl move_base replacement changing launch file. install spqrel_navigation, follow instructions web site. spqrel_navigation modules fully integrated patrolling_sim. select spqrel_navigation ros navigation module start_experiment.py GUI run_exp.sh script. SUPPORT EXTENDED STAGE API NOTE: Extended API stage customized versions stage stageros. https://github.com/iocchi/stage_ros details). extended API control activation GUI elements Stage footprints, simulation speedup, screenshots). Set Custom Stage flag true run_exp.sh script activate functions.

name: python-aniso8601
description: Another ISO 8601 parser for Python
readme: aniso8601 ISO parser Python Features Pure Python implementation Python support extra dependencies Logical behavior Parse time, datetime.time Parse date, datetime.date Parse datetime, datetime.datetime Parse duration, datetime.timedelta Parse interval, tuple dates datetimes Parse repeating interval, date datetime generator UTC offset represented fixed-offset tzinfo regular expressions Installation recommended installation method pip: pip install aniso8601 Alternatively, download source (git repository hosted Bitbucket) install python setup.py install Parsing datetimes parse typical ISO datetime string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_datetime('1977-06-10T12:00:00Z') datetime.datetime(1977, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbd90>) Alternative delimiters space: aniso8601.parse_datetime('1977-06-10 delimiter=' datetime.datetime(1977, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbf50>) UTC offsets supported: aniso8601.parse_datetime('1979-06-05T08:00:00-08:00') datetime.datetime(1979, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbf50>) UTC offset returned datetime naive: aniso8601.parse_datetime('1983-01-22T08:00:00') datetime.datetime(1983, Parsing dates parse date represented ISO string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_date('1984-04-23') datetime.date(1984, Basic format supported aniso8601.parse_date('19840423') datetime.date(1984, parse date ISO week date format: aniso8601.parse_date('1986-W38-1') datetime.date(1986, parse ISO ordinal date: aniso8601.parse_date('1988-132') datetime.date(1988, Parsing times parse time formatted ISO string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_time('11:31:14') datetime.time(11, basic format supported: aniso8601.parse_time('113114') datetime.time(11, UTC offset times: aniso8601.parse_time('17:18:19-02:30') datetime.time(17, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fad82c50>) aniso8601.parse_time('171819Z') datetime.time(17, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f44fadbbd90>) Reduced accuracy supported: aniso8601.parse_time('21:42') datetime.time(21, aniso8601.parse_time('22') datetime.time(22, decimal fraction allowed lowest order element ISO formatted time: aniso8601.parse_time('22:33.5') datetime.time(22, aniso8601.parse_time('23.75') datetime.time(23, Parsing durations parse duration formatted ISO string: import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_duration('P1Y2M3DT4H54M6S') datetime.timedelta(428, Reduced accuracy supported: aniso8601.parse_duration('P1Y') datetime.timedelta(365) decimal fraction allowed lowest order element: aniso8601.parse_duration('P1YT3.5M') datetime.timedelta(365, decimal fraction comma full-stop: aniso8601.parse_duration('P1YT3,5M') datetime.timedelta(365, Parsing duration combined date time supported aniso8601.parse_duration('P0001-01-02T01:30:5') datetime.timedelta(397, Parsing intervals parse interval start import aniso8601 aniso8601.parse_interval('2007-03-01T13:00:00/2008-05-11T15:30:00') (datetime.datetime(2007, datetime.datetime(2008, Intervals start time duration supported: aniso8601.parse_interval('2007-03-01T13:00:00Z/P1Y2M10DT2H30M') (datetime.datetime(2007, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f698d44d110>), datetime.datetime(2008, tzinfo=<aniso8601.UTCOffset object 0x7f698d44d110>)) duration duration time: aniso8601.parse_interval('P1M/1981-04-05') (datetime.date(1981, datetime.date(1981, Notice result parse order earliest latest. sorted intervals required, simply 'sorted' keyword shown sorted(aniso8601.parse_interval('P1M/1981-04-05')) [datetime.date(1981, datetime.date(1981, Repeating intervals supported return generator: aniso8601.parse_repeating_interval('R3/1981-04-05/P1D') <generator object date_generator 0x7f698cdefc80> list(aniso8601.parse_repeating_interval('R3/1981-04-05/P1D')) [datetime.date(1981, datetime.date(1981, datetime.date(1981, Repeating intervals allowed reverse direction: list(aniso8601.parse_repeating_interval('R2/PT1H2M/1980-03-05T01:01:00')) [datetime.datetime(1980, datetime.datetime(1980, Unbounded intervals allowed (Python result aniso8601.parse_repeating_interval('R/PT1H2M/1980-03-05T01:01:00') result.next() datetime.datetime(1980, result.next() datetime.datetime(1980, Python result aniso8601.parse_repeating_interval('R/PT1H2M/1980-03-05T01:01:00') next(result) datetime.datetime(1980, next(result) datetime.datetime(1980, Note convert name: tornado
description: Tornado is a Python web framework and asynchronous networking library, originally developed at FriendFeed. http://www.tornadoweb.org/
readme: Tornado Web Server Tornado Python web framework asynchronous networking library, originally developed FriendFeed. non-blocking network Tornado scale tens thousands open connections, making ideal long polling, WebSockets, applications require long-lived connection user. Upgrade notes Tornado backports.ssl_match_hostname package installed running Tornado Python installed automatically pip easy_install. Quick links Documentation Source (github) Mailing list Stack Overflow Wiki world simple world" web app Tornado: {.sourceCode .python} import tornado.ioloop import tornado.web class MainHandler(tornado.web.RequestHandler): def get(self): self.write(“Hello, world”) application tornado.web.Application([ MainHandler), “main”: application.listen(8888) tornado.ioloop.IOLoop.instance().start() Tornado's asynchronous features; simple chat room. Installation Automatic installation: pip install tornado Tornado listed PyPI installed pip easy_install. Node rosrun pcdfilter_pa pcdfilter_pa_node roslaunch pcdfilter_pa pcdfilter_pa.launch Input Output Topics Topic Type Description ”~/in_cloud” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Input pointcloud type. ”~/in_cloud_old” sensor_msgs/PointCloud Input pointloud type. converted pointcloud type. ”~/in_laser” sensor_msgs/LaserScan Input single laser scan. converted pointcloud type package “laser_geometry”. ”~/out_cloud” sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 Output filtering node. topics remapped parameters Services Service Type Description ”~/filter” pcdfilter_pa/PcdFilterPaCloud Forced filtering service call. throttling input/output cloud part service message. ”~/add_filters” pcdfilter_pa/PcdFilterPaFilter Adding filters. filters ”~/change_filters” pcdfilter_pa/PcdFilterPaFilter Adding filters, removing filters adding. ”~/enable” std_srvs/Empty Disables node. disconnects node input messages. ”~/disable” std_srvs/Empty Enables node. Parameters Filter Parameter Type Description “~/filters” vector strings pointcloud filters array strings. general settings Parameter Type Description “~/skip_count” integer Input throttling. Number skipped messages processed message. “~/skip_time” double Input throttling. Time intervall seconds. processed message, intervall starts input messages skipped. “~/tf_lookup_time” double Maximum time seconds waiting specific transform. “~/buffer_pointcloud” bool Flag buffering received pointcloud. test filters resending pointcloud. “~/debugging” bool Flag enabling extented output. “~/enabled” bool Flag setting start behaviour. node filtering startup, laser Parameter Type Description “~/laser_nan_replacement_value” double nan-value represented laser scan, obstacle range”. parameters replace values fixed number. topics services Parameter Type Description “~/topic_in_cloud” string input topic pointclouds. “~/topic_in_cloud_old” string input topic pointclouds. “~/topic_in_laser” string input topic laser scans. “~/topic_out_cloud” string output topic filtered pointcloud. config file. parameters default Filter Definition definition single filter string. [<inversion>]<type> <separator> <dimensions> [<tf_offset>] [<tf2_offset>]] <comment>] inversion (optional) Inversion Description normal behaviour; points volume filtered points reversed behaviour; points volume points filtered type Type Description Parameters ids “cube” sidelength parameter “sphere” radius parameter “block” cuboid sidelengths direction parameters “cylinder” radius height parameters “link” cylinder centered tfs; radius overshoot parameters “cone” cone headed fist pointing height ratio (radius/height) parameters separator Seperator Description current filter required; missing filtering aborted output pointcloud generated current filter skipped; missing current filter applied; case filtering output process continued control long filter wait exists parameter “~/tf_lookup_time” Node. dimensions Number dimensions depends type current filter(see dimension needed, cube. dimensions set cuboid. dimension single number seperated spaces. ROS frame filtered objects. tf_offset (optional) additional offset relative frame Order parameters: Missing parameters set set set sqrt(1 tf2_offset (optional) valid type “link” “cone”. description tf_offset). comment character give short comment. name: rsync_ros
description: An actionlib interface for Rsync
readme: #rsync_ros ##Overview package brings Rsync operations (file transfer syncronization) ROS form actionlib interface. Making convenient transfer files machine ROS. Progress transfers published action feedback. ##Potential Cases RsyncAction goal robot reached state. file logfile bagfile) transferred remote fileserver storage. Rsync? Rsync fast versatile utllity file transfer, widely backups mirroring, improved copy command everyday copy locally, to/from host remote shell, offering large number options control aspect behavior. famous delta-transfer algorithm, reduces amount data network sending differences source files existing files destination. ##Usage start server rosrun rsync_ros rsync_server_node.py ####CLI Actionlib Client scripts/rsync_client_example.py, simple client based actionlib_tutorials simple actionlib client. #####Local transfer rosrun rsync_ros rsync_client_example.py -avzp ~/a_file.txt ~/file_dest.txt #####Remote transfer set ssh keys remote machine, tutorial rosrun rsync_ros rsync_client_example.py -avzp ~/a_file.txt ssh_user@remote_host:~/file_dest.txt ##Choosing Rysnc Arguments server passes arguments listed rsync_args Rsync, Rsync arguments tested. you’re unfamiliar RSync, manual full list arguments. TL;DR -avz argument works copy operations, -avz --delete-source-files arguments move operations. argument (Partial Transfer) particuarly dealing unreliable (wireless) connections. ##Simple Python Actionlib #!/usr/bin/env python import rospy import roslib; roslib.load_manifest('rsync_ros') import actionlib import rsync_ros.msg import sys rospy.init_node('rsync_client') source_path '~/a_file.txt' dest_path 'SSH_USER@192.168.0.1:~/file_dest.txt' Create SimpleActionClient, passing RsyncAction constructor. client actionlib.SimpleActionClient('rsync_ros', rsync_ros.msg.RsyncAction) Wait action server started started listening goals. client.wait_for_server() Create goal send action server. goal rsync_ros.msg.RsyncGoal(rsync_args=['-avzp'], source_path=source_path, destination_path=dest_path) Sends goal action server. client.send_goal(goal) Waits server finish performing action. client.wait_for_result() result action result client.get_result() Prints result executing action rospy.loginfo("Successful Transfer: {}".format(result.sync_success)) RsyncResult ##Rsync Action Definition #Goal string[] rsync_args #List Rsync command line arguments ['-avzh', '--partial'] string source_path "/home/user/file_to_sync.txt" string destination_path "ssh_username@192.168.0.1:/home/user/file_destination.txt" #Result bool sync_success #returns true file(s) synced correctly #Feedback float32 percent_complete int64 transfer_rate bytes/second
